recompile curobo to solve ABI incompatibility, remaining checkpoint_id=5000 path issue needs correction ✅ gadget Homepage-Based student discovery strategy implementation (2026-03-15) — Implemented homepage_discovery.py module (~200 lines), 4-stage discovery strategy (homepage priority + co-authorship supplement), multi-strategy URL discovery (S2 homepage field+LLM suggestion+-homepage parameter), HTMLParser text extraction, 2MB limit+7-day cache TTL, solving the fundamental problem of S2 co-author analysis being completely ineffective for top scholars Problems and Solutions 1. Drop Skill: The OSC controller actively maintains the EEF position (impedance control) in env.step(), and after the claw is opened, the object is held by a finger and cannot fall freely [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Bypass the controller and directly set MuJoCo qpos/qvel, then call mujoco.mj_step() to run 15 physical steps for initial separation, followed by standard control cycles
2. MIHD sample-crossing embedding comparison ineffective: per-section independent HVG selection + independent PCA fitting leads to non-comparable feature spaces, the conclusion that ‘PCA is better than STAIG’ indicates a methodological error [MIHD] (2026-03-15) Solution: Use the raw_shared intersection of shared HVGs (1137 genes) and unified processing as the correct baseline. Load directly from the original HDF5 instead of relying on per-section caches (there is an integerization bug in var_names)
3. Stack name parsing silent failure: stack.yaml uses cubeA/cubeB, MuJoCo’s actual name is cubeA_main, _sim_body_name2id returns -1, Python negative index causes all task phase detections to be misjudged as pre_reach [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Fix the body name segment, add a main/_body0 suffix backtracking logic in sim_body_name2id, and handle failure output by checking WARNING instead of silently returning -1
4. pi05 training is 33% slower than openpi (20h vs 15h), intuition suggests hardware differences, but the root cause is unknown [VLA-RoboTwin/pi05] (2026-03-11) Solution: By comparing parallel sub-proxies at the software level (pyproject.toml/uv.lock/wandb logs), identify the main reason as JAX version differences (0.5.0 vs 0.5.3). XLA compiler optimization has accumulated over time; use uv override-dependencies to resolve lerobot torch version upper limit conflicts
5. curobo pre-compiled.so and torch 2.7.1 ABI incompatibility (undefined symbol), JIT recompilation failed due to non-standard CUDA header path in conda [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-12)Solution: Set CUDA_HOME to the root directory of the conda environment, set CPATH to targets/x86_64-linux/include/, and use pip install -e to recompile from source. 6. Severe imbalance in online quota generation: premature_release naturally captures 7233 items, with 7 types completely at zero. The distribution of policy behavior is uneven and uncontrollable [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-09) Solution: Establish an offline injection architecture: first collect trajectories during a full rollout, index injectable points offline, then selectively inject according to quotas; skip over已满 types.
7. CalendarPro intention misclassification: no confidence threshold (0.52 is also considered a valid classification), time expressions are misjudged by keyword routing, short confirmation words have no context understanding, Chinese tokens are estimated with a 3-fold deviation [CalendarPro] (2026-03-15) Solution: Add per-routing confidence thresholds (0.40-0.60), introduce a keyword scorer combined with an embedding routing method of 70/30 ratio. Split the system prompt into base and 11 fragments for injection as needed, and estimate Chinese tokens at ×1.5.
8. S2 co-author analysis is completely ineffective for top scholars (Levine/Abbeel/Finn, etc.) (all depth-2 fields are empty). There is a serious name ambiguity issue with Xiaolong Wang/Shuran Song [gadget] (2026-03-15) Solution: Reconstruct the strategy to prioritize homepage-first: preferentially capture student lists from professors’ personal pages, and co-authorship should only serve as a supplement. Use multiple strategies for URL discovery. Mark warnings for name ambiguities and recommend using S2 authorId for precise queries.
9. Incorrect assumption in VLA context replay architecture: a complete N-1 frame replay mechanism was designed, but most VLAs do not have a context window. This overhead is an ineffective design [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Reconstruct ContextReplayEngine into InjectionEngine, directly restoring the injected frame sim state. Limit data sources to MimicGen demo data, which is more controllable.
10. RTX 2080 Ti + PyTorch 2.9.0 triggers cuBLAS CUBLAS_STATUS_EXECUTION_FAILED for high-dimensional tensors with N>3500 [MIHD] (2026-03-09) Solution: First project to hidden_dim(128) using Linear projection, then index neighbors (to avoid high-dimensional large tensors entering cuBLAS). Change InfoNCE to a mini-batch comparison loss (batch_size=512).
11. MCP Server tool returns file paths, AI cannot consume the content directly [gadget] (2026-03-09) Solution: Reconstruct the tool to call underlying functions directly without using cmd_* wrappers, returning complete content (markdown/JSON). Control file writing through save parameters.
12. Incorrect assumption in pi0.py regarding LeRobot’s internal behavior: inferred shape=(1,) features are kept in b,1 format and code is modified. In reality, LeRobot DataLoader will squeeze to b, resulting in a mismatch in shape during training [VLA-RoboTwin/pi05] (2026-03-15) Solution: Verify the actual shape by running training and observing logs (‘aux_targets[…]: (32,)@float32’), restore the original[:, None] and jnp.stack operations.
Learning Outcomes Architecture The necessary prerequisite for comparing embeddings across samples is shared feature space: independent HVG selection per section + independent PCA fitting equals dual irrelevance. An effective baseline must use a shared HVG intersection + joint processing, or a foundation model with fixed pre-trained weights. There is a fundamental conflict between direct state operations in MuJoCo and the feedback controller (OSC): sim.forward() only updates kinematics, while mujoco.mj_step() advances dynamics and bypasses the controller. The simulation injection design must clearly choose one path. The semantic split of error types should be based on ‘whether the recovery strategy is the same’ rather than ‘whether the injection mechanism is the same’: the detection conditions and recovery logic for drop_in_transit/drop_at_wrong_place/drop_with_interaction are completely different. Even if the injection actions are the same, they must be modeled separately. The semantic router architecture has a flaw: embedding nearest neighbors always produce results that cannot express ‘uncertainty’. Combining confidence thresholds + alternative LLMs + keyword scorers is the most practical repair model, which can be applied to all vector retrieval-based classification systems (RAG routing, tool selection, etc.). MCP tools should aim to consume AI as their primary goal: return complete content, with file writing being an optional side effect. General benchmarks should not assume that the model has a context window. InjectionEngine directly restoring the sim state is more versatile than context replay. For top scholars (500+ papers), analysis of co-author frequencies cannot identify students - the first-author signal is diluted by many collaborators. Professors’ homepages explicitly list students, which is a factor of reliability. The citation graph (forward + backward) is a core feature of research toolchains. ‘Relevance’ and ’number of citations/popularity’ should be handled independently. Offline injection architecture is better than online quota systems for building balanced error scenario datasets: decouple ’exploring injectability’ from ’executing injections’, allowing precise control over the number of each type of error; online natural capture is greatly affected by policy behavior distribution and cannot control type balance. Debugging Upgrading to a new JAX version (0.5.0→0.5.3) can improve training speed by about 33%. The cumulative effect of XLA compiler optimization cannot be ignored. In the conda environment, compile CUDA extensions: set CUDA_HOME to the root directory of the conda environment, and set CPATH to envs//targets/x86_64-linux/include/ (not /usr/local/cuda/include/); after upgrading torch to a new version, all .so files that depend on torch C++ ABI need to be recompiled. Assumptions about the internal behavior of third-party frameworks must be verified through actual running: LeRobot automatically squeezes single-scalar feature into batch_size for DataLoader when shape=(1,) is inferred. This inference is unreliable. The actual effective values in training configuration need to be verified from wandb logs. Code default values may be overridden by CLI parameters. Monitoring GPUs within K8s containers: scan/proc//fd/ for devices linked to/dev/nvidia*, and prioritize reading CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. Processes that open all GPU devices but do not occupy memory are usually monitoring tools, which can be filtered based on this information. Silent failures are the most dangerous bug mode: body_xpos[-1] negative index always returns the same position in two cubes; integerizing cached var_names results in zero intersection of gene names. Any parsing failure should immediately trigger a WARNING instead of returning a sentinel value. Perform integrity checks before using cached data. Domain Knowledge scGPT independent benchmark (Genome Biology 2025) confirms that zero-shot is inferior to PCA/scVI; scGPT-spatial only compares with weak baselines (ARI≈0.30-0.40). SOTA (GraphST, ARI≈0.55-0.63) was not included, and there is no independent third-party verification. When evaluating new methods, it is necessary to verify whether their baseline is the current SOTA. CALVIN evaluation is a purely online simulation that does not read episode data at all. Only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml is needed. The 1.3GB dataset can be compressed into a 600KB eval-only version. A list of humanoid AI scholars and their mentors: Mingyu Ding←Jitendra Malik, Ruoshi Liu←Carl Vondrick, Xiaolong Wang←Abhinav Gupta, Shuran Song←Thomas Funkhouser, Yunzhu Li←Antonio Torralba, Yuke Zhu←Li Fei-Fei - presenting a systematic group of top-level perception/robotics mentors to humanoid AI. Stream matching is becoming the mainstream action decoding architecture for VLA. Pi0 time convention: t=1 for pure noise → t=0 for target action. Using adaRMS to inject time conditions in Pi0.5 is better than simple splicing. In VLA-assisted tasks, isolating the main task gradient with stop_gradient is a safe starting point. Tools Prompt engineering injection strategy: Split the system prompt into base (~50 lines) + intent-specific fragments (dynamically injected by category), which can reduce token consumption by 40-60%. The token density for Chinese characters is about 6 times that of English characters (1.5 tokens/character vs 0.25 tokens/character). Without correction, the context length will be systematically underestimated. For projects with multiple tools coexisting, the output directory should be organized according to ‘file type priority’ (outputs/reports/summarize/, not summarize/reports/). The.gitignore can be simplified to a single line outputs/. The Python re-export shim mode (only containing from x import y; all=[…]) is an elegant migration method that remains backward compatible. The PubMed esearch→efetch two-step E-utilities API can index metadata of subscription journals such as Nature/Cell/Science for free. The bioRxiv API is also open, and neither requires new dependencies (urllib.request). Verifying the feasibility of a pipeline with a small batch is better than using a full load. Notes on AI Usage Effective Patterns:
✓ Parallel sub-proxies accelerate multi-dimensional code analysis: rely on version differences for diagnosis. Start 3+ sub-proxies simultaneously during code library exploration to cover different file sets, significantly reducing analysis time. ✓ Goal-driven delegation + iterative debugging loop: Users provide clear termination conditions (‘fix until no errors’). AI runs iteratively independently → reports an error → makes the smallest modification possible. There is an built-in error-correction mechanism. ✓ Deep code library exploration identifies architectural challenges: CrossModalEnhancer actively detects degradation problems in single spots KV (each spot has only one vector), and proposes a solution to build KV sequences from spatial neighbors. ✓ Hack sys.path → progressive refactoring of common/packages: The re-export shim mode remains backward compatible while eliminating duplicate code. ✓ Verifying the feasibility of a small batch pipeline is better than using a full load (207 scenarios reveal 5 systematic defects). End-to-end integration testing reveals implicit dependencies at the pipeline level more effectively than unit testing. Limitations:
✗ Insufficient ability to reflect on experimental conclusions: Directly attribute numerical results without actively questioning the validity of the experimental design. The methodological flaws in MIHD embedding need to be corrected by users through external triggers. ✗ Silent failure modes were not actively detected: Names of Stack bodies returned -1, negative indices in Python, integerizing cached var_names. All these require user discovery due to lack of integrity checks. ✗ Over-engineering and incorrect architectural assumptions: VLA context replay is based on the wrong assumption that ‘all VLAs need a context window’. Incorrect inferences about LeRobot’s shape behavior were made and code was modified, which requires user correction or verification. ✗ Insufficient ability to actively question the applicability of methodologies: S2 students continue to debug the code logic when they discover that it is ineffective. They do not actively question the boundaries of the methodology itself and need guidance from users before turning to the homepage solution. ✗ Weak ability to handle name ambiguities in Semantic Scholar: There is a lack of active entity disambiguation for common Chinese-English translations. LLM analysis cannot automatically identify ambiguous researchers. ✗ The signature was not verified before using APIs: The FastMCP version parameter and conda –no-banner were discovered to be incompatible only after running the program. Next Week’s Plans Next week (2026-W12) focus: ① Implement ErrorRecoveryBenchmark v5.1 - Complete generation of D0 scenarios for 5 fixed skills (targeting 600+ items), progress with the reconstruction of InjectionEngine, speed limit on motion, and collection pipeline for humanoid examples using keyboard teleoperation. Milestone: Start training recovery strategies by April 1st; ② VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 - Re-convert LeRobot dataset (including 5 new fields like manip_progress), start four groups of auxiliary experiments for training and comparative analysis, and conduct formal strategy evaluation after correcting checkpoint_id in eval.sh; ③ MIHD - Complete the diagnosis of the raw_shared baseline and draw conclusions on methodological fixes, solve the problem of 151676 GPUs being retrained (fixed PyTorch version), and evaluate the full GPU pipeline of CrossModalEnhancer; ④ gadget/research - Deploy portraits of 7 scholars to the Hugo research section, and explicitly require English quotation marks in prompts to completely eliminate JSON pollution generated by LLMs; ⑤ UniVLA - Complete the verification process for CALVIN evaluation (in the –single_gpu mode).

summary: This week, six projects were carried out concurrently on three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. The projects include the following key tasks: ① MIHD spatial transcriptomics identified fundamental flaws in the cross-sample embedding methodology (independent processing per section leads to non-comparable feature spaces), and efforts are underway to fix these issues. ② ErrorRecoveryBenchmark improved from bug fixes to a comprehensive scale-up, covering 13 skills/29 subtypes. The project also addressed the issue of objects not falling when dropping skills, and after identifying limitations in online quota architectures, a new approach for offline injection was established. ③ VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 completed the entire process from environment setup, training performance optimization (JAX version alignment, 33% speed improvement) to data collection, and auxiliary task experiments. ④ The gadget toolchain upgraded its architecture to include MCP Server, common/shared packages, and unified output directories. Research profilers enabled researchers to prioritize student discoveries on scholar pages. ⑤ CalendarPro underwent comprehensive optimization in 7 stages, with all 230 tests passing. Token consumption was reduced by 40-60%. ⑥ The gadget research toolchain integrated citation graph analysis and completed profiles of 7 renowned AI scholars.

summary: The fully optimized implementation plan of CalendarPro has been completed. We have adopted a parallel implementation plan that consists of 7 stages (having passed 230 tests). We have integrated various tools, enabling the retrieval of papers, performance analysis, and citation information to be processed within a unified command-line interface. The shared tool code has been extracted and stored in a common folder, or packaged and saved, in order to avoid duplicating more than 400 lines of code. All output directories have been organized into a structured hierarchical file system. In addition, by collaborating with 13 top researchers in the fields of robotics/a artificial intelligence, we have established a systematic academic research tracking mechanism.

summary: In the process of developing multiple projects, it is necessary to carry out a series of document cleanup tasks, as well as spatial transcriptomics analysis, optimization of robot learning, and troubleshooting of environmental issues that exist between various code repositories. title: Bug Journal 2026-03-10

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-09 Daily Overview “Completed Work: Parallel progress on five main directions across three devices: (1) Development of core algorithms for MIHD spatial transcriptomics project (cross-sample structure evaluation + cross-modal enhancement architecture), (2) Development of VLA/robot learning systems (documentation, data pipeline, theoretical analysis), (3) Transformation of gadget tool architecture (CLI→MCP server), (4) Cross-project integration (CalendarPro automated workflow), (5) Enhancement of research tools (multi-data sources + production-level logs)” “Completion Methods: Used WWL graph core to establish spatial structure evaluation benchmarks, designed cross-attention mechanism based on spatial neighborhoods (addressing CUDA hardware compatibility issues), systematically researched foundation model performance, created MCP protocol wrapper layer for tool exposure, implemented cross-environment calls through conda subprocesses, extended research_scout to three academic sources (arXiv/bioRxiv/PubMed), established production-level log system” “Impact: Established reusable cross-sample spatial evaluation tools and cross-modal enhancement methods, revealed key findings that scGPT foundation model is inferior to traditional methods, made gadget tools accessible to all AI agents, implemented CalendarPro automated daily research and summary workflows, expanded research coverage from arXiv single source to three academic sources and established a complete error tracking mechanism” DCC “Completed Work: Full process of MIHD spatial transcriptomics project: cross-sample RM-IDEAL experiments and visualization (151673-151508/151676), design and implementation of CrossModalEnhancer with multiple rounds of CUDA debugging, maintenance of CLAUDE.md, research on scGPT literature” “Completion Methods: Running large-scale experiments in HPC environment (3639 Spot instances), using conda environment management + multiple rounds of CUDA error debugging (最终采用mini-batch workaround and CPU training fallback), performing parallel web searches and WebFetch for benchmark data acquisition, generating spatial heatmap visualizations” “Impact: Produced complete cross-sample evaluation reports (7 spatial heatmaps + Chinese technical documentation), delivered functional CrossModalEnhancer module (integrated into Fusion.py), obtained key insights on scGPT performance (zero-shot inferior to traditional methods), identified PyTorch CUDA version compatibility issues and established CPU training fallback process” TzJsDesktop “Completed Work: Full-stack integration and tool enhancement work: created workspace documentation for 5 projects, implementation of gadget MCP server (9 tools), CalendarPro-gadget integration bridge (13 tests), enhancement of research_scout (log system + multi-data sources, over 500 new lines of code), maintenance of documentation updates” “Completion Methods: Used parallel agent orchestration for code library exploration, systematic file-by-file implementation and verification at each stage, implemented cross-repo integration through subprocess isolation, conducted comprehensive testing (import checks, CLI validation, log file verification), used urllib.request to create zero-dependency API client” “Impact: Established unified development environment and clear documentation, made gadget tools accessible to all AI agents in the workspace, implemented CalendarPro automated daily research workflows, transformed research_scout from arXiv single source to three-source system with production-level observability, maintained synchronization of all tool documentation and code” tianhe “Completed Work: Progress on multiple VLA/robot projects: creation of UniVLA documentation, troubleshooting CALVIN environment issues, data conversion from RoboTwin to LeRobot, theoretical analysis of Pi0 flow matching” “Completion Methods: Created comprehensive CLAUDE.md (build/test commands, architecture overview), diagnosed DNS parsing failures (Kubernetes environment), implemented convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py (14-DOF, 50 episodes, 11,459 frames), detailed conditional flow matching (Beta(1.5,1) time sampling + Euler integration)” “Impact: Established documentation standards for complex VLA code libraries, supported reuse of demo_clean data for pi0.5 training, clarified implementation details of flow matching for robot action generation” Completed core functions of MIHD spatial transcriptomics (cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation, CrossModalEnhancer cross-modal enhancement, scGPT literature research), advanced multiple VLA/robot projects (UniVLA documentation, RoboTwin data conversion, flow matching analysis), implemented MCP serverization and CalendarPro integration for gadget tools, enhanced research_scout multi-data source support
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Run 151673-151508 cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark tests — Used PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion embeddings to execute benchmarks, comparing spatial niche structure similarity between two DLPFC parts. Detailed correlation metrics (Spearman ρ) were generated for all 7 organizational layers. ✅ Implement CrossModalEnhancer module — Created model/CrossModalEnhancer.py with CrossModalAttentionBlock and CrossModalEnhancer classes (about 350 lines), supporting 3 modes (gene→image, image→gene, bidirectional). Integrated into Fusion.py scheduler. ✅ Debug CUDA/cuBLAS compatibility issues — Resolved crashes during large matrix operations due to PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS on RTX 2080 Ti. Final solution was to use mini-batch (512 samples) InfoNCE loss to avoid N×N full matrix backpropagation. ✅ Research scGPT clustering benchmark literature — Conducted systematic web searches and literature extraction to evaluate scGPT and scGPT spatial clustering performance. Found that zero-shot scGPT performs worse than traditional methods (PCA, scVI), while scGPT spatial models lack independent SOTA comparisons. ✅ Create CLAUDE.md documentation for UniVLA repository — Provided detailed documentation covering build commands (torchrun, accelerate), code checks (black/ruff), training phases (LAM, VLA pre-training, fine-tuning), evaluation (LIBERO, CALVIN, R2R, SimplerEnv) and architecture overview (VLM→PrismaticVLM→OpenVLA, visual/LLM base models, action segmentation, draccus configuration, RLDS process). ✅ Refactor MCP tools to return content — Modified 5 MCP tools (summarize_export/merge, research_report, benchmark_run/report) to return complete content (markdown/JSON), allowing selection of save parameters and bypassing cmd_* wrapper layers for file I/O control. ✅ Convert gadget tool to MCP server — Created mcp_server.py exposing 9 tools (summarize_export/merge/list_dates, research_search/report/list_projects, benchmark_run/report/system_info) and implemented stdout capture and SystemExit handling. ✅ Integrate gadget tool into CalendarPro — Built an external tool integration layer (src/tools/), implemented asynchronous subprocess execution through conda run, created ResearchScoutTool and DailySummaryTool, established a background service with cron scheduling capabilities, and wrote 13 unit tests. ✅ Add bioRxiv and PubMed support to research_scout — Implemented search_biorxiv() and search_pubmed(), using urllib.request (no additional dependencies required), added multi-source orchestration functionality, unified caching mechanisms, expanded CLI features, and retained backward compatibility for paper_id/arxiv_id processing. ✅ Generate RM-IDEAL spatial heatmap visualizations — Modified_viz_rm_ideal.py script to generate 7 PNG files (one for each niche label), showing real data, RM-IDEAL scores, and embedding similarity for comparison between 151673↔151508. ✅ Write comprehensive cross-sample patch query documentation (Chinese) — Created docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_PATCH_QUERY_REPORT.md document, including complete methodology (WWL graph core, Wasserstein distance, dual-path evaluation), code paths, data flows, result analysis, and reproducibility guidelines. ❌ Use STAIG fusion for MIHD cross-sample patch visualization — Implemented patch query and visualization using UNI2+PCA+STAIG_fusion embeddings from 151673→151676. Visualization functionality was blocked due to GPU CUDA compatibility issues (PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 incompatible with CUDA 13.1 driver). CPU training served as an alternative solution. ✅ Convert RoboTwin demo_clean to LeRobot format — Created convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py script to convert 50 episodes (11,459 frames) from demo_clean’s 14-DOF HDF5 format to LeRobot dataset structure, stored in RoboTwin/data/place_dual_shoes_lerobot. ✅ Add log system to research_scout — Implemented RotatingFileHandler (5MB size, 3 backups), migrated approximately 77 print statements to logger, added warnings for empty fields and zero scores, enhanced error handling for API calls and caching operations. ✅ Design CrossModalEnhancer architecture — Designed a cross-modal enhancement module using spatial k-NN neighbors as K/V sequences to avoid degradation of single-label attention mechanisms. Designed symmetric InfoNCE losses and residual connections. “✅ Analyze Pi0 flow matching implementation — Explained conditional flow matching in pi0.py: linear interpolation path x_t = t·noise + (1-t)·actions, Beta(1.5,1) time sampling, MSE loss‖v_t - u_t‖², using Euler method to sample 10 steps from t=1 to 0.” ✅ Analyze CALVIN training data flow — Detailed explanation of how finetune_calvin.py loads auto_lang_ann.npy, episode_.npz files, extracts rgb_static/rel_actions/robot_obs, and transmits through LAM encoder and ActionDecoder. Implementation and Fixes - ✅ Create CALVIN dataset extraction script — Created extract_eval_files.py file to extract only the necessary evaluation files (validation files/.hydra/merged_config.yaml, metadata) from the complete dataset, reducing file size from 1.3GB to 600KB ✅ Create workplace-level CLAUDE.md documentation — Analyzed 5 independent projects (CalendarPro, OCR, gadget, get-shit-done, openclaw), created unified CLAUDE.md documentation with quick reference commands and architectural highlights “✅ Update research_scout.py’s CLAUDE.md documentation — Identified and recorded previously unrecorded features: author search (–author flag, search_arxiv_author), init –from-overview (creation of projects based on LLM), increased line count from approximately 2500 to approximately 2650 lines, added author search caching mode” ✅ Update CLAUDE.md documentation containing cross-modal and RM-IDEAL content — Added CrossModalEnhancer to core modules, added RM-IDEAL benchmark commands to common commands, increased cross-sample document references, and enriched descriptions of utils (rm_ideal.py, niche_utils.py) ✅ Simplify CLAUDE.md module list — Reduced architecture section of each module from approximately 30 lines to approximately 10 lines, highlighting non-obvious module relationships. Retained all key architectural patterns “❌ Solve CALVIN environment installation issues — Diagnosed DNS parsing failures (Errno -3), preventing pip from accessing pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn or pypi.org in Kubernetes environment. Identified proxy configuration (http_proxy=localhost:9997) but failed to solve the problem” Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. RTX 2080 Ti vulnerability in PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS caused crashes during backpropagation for large matrix operations (comparative loss required 3639×3639 logits). Multiple architectural rewrites were attempted before finding the root cause. Solution: Redesigned the comparison loss function, using mini-batch sampling (512 samples per batch) to avoid full N×N matrix calculations. Added gradient clipping and LayerNorm for stability. For cross-sample visualization tasks, when GPU incompatibility was confirmed, switched to CPU training as an alternative solution (PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 vs CUDA 13.1 driver).
Key Insight: Model architecture alone cannot solve GPU hardware/driver compatibility issues—algorithmic solutions are required at the loss function level. Incompatibility between PyTorch CUDA version and driver version can lead to “illegal memory access” errors, which can be detected through basic tests but fail in complex training loops. For medium-scale training (3460 sample points), using CPU as an alternative is a valid strategy.
2. MCP server stdout corruption: gadget tools heavily use print(), but MCP uses stdout as a JSON-RPC transport method Solution: Created capture_stdout() context manager to redirect print() output, wrapped all tool calls in safe_call(), captured stdout/stderr and converted SystemExit to RuntimeError, returning the captured logs in the _logs field of the JSON response.
Key Insight: Isolating stdout is crucial when integrating existing CLI tools into protocol-based systems. The context manager provides a clean, recoverable way to redirect output without modifying the original code
3. research_scout hard-codes arXiv_id throughout the codebase, hindering multi-source expansion Solution: Introduced paper_id as a standard ID with source field, retained arXiv_id as an backward-compatible alias, created _paper_id()/_paper_url() helper functions, updated prompts to use paper_id, and made the merging logic check both fields when matching LLM responses.
Key Insight: Introducing new abstractions from single-source to multi-source expansions while keeping old fields as aliases. Helper functions centralize ID/URL logic, making future data source additions simple
4. In cross-sample queries for 151673-151508, Layer_3 has low correlation (ρ=-0.21/-0.36), while Layer_1/4/5 show high positive correlations (ρ>0.4) Solution: Analysis showed that Layer_3 is the largest DLPFC layer with high internal heterogeneity, making embeddings unable to capture unified spatial structures. Smaller and well-defined layers (Layer_1: ρ=0.62, Layer_4: ρ=0.66) perform best.
Key Insight: The scale and internal heterogeneity of organizational layers are crucial for capturing spatial structure in embeddings. Independent layers with clear boundaries perform better cross-sample matching than large heterogeneous regions
5. When each sample has only one gene vector and one image vector, single-label cross-attention degrades to linear projection Solution: Used spatial k-NN neighbors to construct K/V sequences, allowing cross-attention to operate in neighborhood contexts. Reused QFormerFusion’s batch neighbor construction infrastructure.
Key Insight: Spatial transcriptomics data provides natural sequential context through neighborhood structures. Utilizing spatial adjacency allows meaningful attention to be achieved without time or batch dimensions
6. RoboTwin demo_clean data (14-DOF, based on directories) is incompatible with existing LeRobot conversion script (16-DOF, based on zip format)Solution: A new script, convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py, was created. This script reads data directly from the data/commands directory, changes ACTION_DIM/STATE_DIM to 14, uses the LeRobotDataset.create() function, and sets the root parameter as an environment variable instead of using the HF_LEROBOT_HOME variable. Key Insight: Changes in the robot data format (number of DOF, storage format) require custom conversion scripts. The directory structure and HDF5 key names are project-specific.
7. Due to JSON parsing failures by the LLM, empty motivation/innovation fields appeared in the research_scout filtering stage. Solution: Empty field detection was added after _screen_papers() and _screen_papers_chunked(). Warnings with counts and DEBUG levels were recorded for each paper. The _try_repair_result() function was enhanced to record the original response preview in case of failures during repairs.
Key Insight: Unintentional.get() backtracking masked data quality issues. Clear verification and logging at the aggregation point (after batch processing) could provide actionable debugging information without complicating the logic for each project.
8. The initial CrossModalEnhancer design output original modal dimensions (image is 1536d), which caused cuBLAS GEMM errors during the 128→1536 projection. Solution: The architecture was redesigned to hide the dimension space output (128d) instead of keeping the large matrix projection problem. While maintaining a compact core output, projection heads were used for contrast loss calculations.
Key Insight: Engineering limitations (GPU failures) may force architectural decisions that differ from ideal semantic designs. The actual system must find a balance between theoretical elegance and implementability.
General Issues 9. The FastMCP constructor rejected the ‘version’ parameter in the user’s conda environment. Solution: Inspect.signature() was used to check the actual constructor. The version parameter was removed, and only ’name’ and ‘instructions’ were passed, allowing the mcp library to handle version management internally.
Key Insight: The SDK API is constantly evolving. When integration issues arise, it’s important to check the actual code signature rather than relying on documentation or assumptions. The FastMCP API of the mcp library has changed over time.
10. The CLAUDE.md documentation is outdated—missing author search functionality, init –from-overview function, and incorrect line count statistics. Solution: The entire codebase was scanned using Grep to find undocumented functions like search_arxiv_author, create_project_from_overview. The line count was verified using wc -l. Six targeted modifications were made to the documentation section to update it with instructions, code structure, key implementation details, and file layout.
Key Insight: In tools that are actively developed, outdated documentation is inevitable. Systematic code scanning (such as grep –author, –from-overview, etc.) is more reliable than manual review.
11. DNS resolution failed in the Kubernetes Pod, preventing pip from accessing PyPI images. Solution: The issue was diagnosed to be the domain name server 10.96.0.10, with a proxy local host of 9997. However, this did not solve the problem—it might be a cluster-level network configuration issue.
Key Insight: The DNS and outbound proxy configurations of the Kubernetes cluster can disrupt the standard pip workflow. It may be necessary for the cluster administrator to intervene or use cached packages.
12. A “Parameter Not Recognized” error occurred when using the –no-banner flag during conda runtime on the user’s conda version. Solution: The –no-banner flag was completely removed. Using conda run without this flag worked normally, and the banner output was sent to stderr (without damaging stdout). Testing showed that using conda run -n calendarpro python -c ‘print(test)’ was feasible.
Key Insight: If certain optional flags improve the clarity of the output but are not necessary in terms of functionality, they should be removed. Bannering in stderr does not affect the protocol based on stdout.
Human Methods vs. AI Methods Strategic Level MCP Tool Design Concept Role Method Human Summarizing/studying insights is “Claude code enhancement skills” — it should return content, not just write files. This is clearly stated as an architectural principle. AI The initially implemented tools were in the form of scripts that wrote files and included metadata, without considering usage patterns in context Difference Analysis: Human users understand the tool-assisted interaction model and workflow. AI implements based on existing script patterns, without considering integration context.
CalendarPro-gadget Integration Method: MCP shares code with child processes Role Method Human Although AI recommended the MCP method, users chose child processes and conda run. Users understand that the gadget tool is a single-layer CLI script (over 2800 lines) and want to avoid any modification of the gadget code. A clear separation was achieved through the boundaries of child processes. AI Initially, AI recommended using the MCP server as the main method, considering it “cleanest” due to native tool discovery capabilities. Only after explicit instructions from users did AI fully adopt the child process method and designed a detailed ToolRunner. Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize maintainability and separation of concerns (zero dependency on other repositories), rather than the “simplicity” of the protocol. AI initially favored the elegance of the protocol. Human choices reflect pragmatic thinking for the long-term development of independent codebases.
Deciding Between GPU or CPU Training After CUDA Errors Role Method Human Immediately questioned “Why use a CPU for training? You can run it on a GPU!” — prioritized using faster hardware and debugging GPU issues. AI After encountering CUDA errors, a conservative CPU training approach was chosen to avoid failure. The root cause of GPU compatibility issues was not thoroughly investigated. Difference Analysis: Humans strongly prefer optimal performance (GPU) and tend to solve problems directly. AI tends to avoid mistakes and chooses safer alternatives (CPU). Human intuition about GPUs working is stronger than initial signs of failure.
CrossModalEnhancer Output Dimension Design Role Method Human Initially planned to specify output dimensions that matched the original mode (image is 1536-dimensional) to maintain semantic space, prioritizing theoretical合理性. AI After encountering cuBLAS errors, it was adjusted to output 128-dimensional data in a hidden dimension space to avoid hardware limitations. Difference Analysis: Humans maintained an ideal architectural design from a theoretical perspective. AI adjusted the architecture based on discovered implementation constraints, prioritizing “working code” when there were conflicts between “working code” and “elegant design”.
Data File Migration Strategy Role Method Human Directly suggested “Just copy and paste it here”—aware that the correct data already existed and used file system moves. AI Planned to delete old data and regenerate from scratch — conservative but resource-intensive when the correct data was available. Difference Analysis: Humans quickly recognized the validity of existing data and chose a direct operating system approach; AI defaulted to regeneration to ensure correctness. The efficiency of humans and the caution of AI formed a contrast.
Implementation Level scGPT Benchmarking Methodology Role Method Human Raised a broad question: “What do people think about ‘searching for existing papers’ in scGPT and clustering benchmarks?” Seeking a comprehensive analysis. AI Broken down into multiple target keyword searches (zero sample, ARI/NMI metrics, specific comparisons), parallel execution of WebSearch and WebFetch, then synthesizing discovery results in a structured summary format. Difference Analysis: Humans posed questions at the conceptual level (“What do people think?”). AI transformed these questions into executable search strategies with specific metrics, datasets, and comparison baselines, then reconstructed conceptual answers.
RM-IDEAL Visualization Requirements Role Method Human Immediately asked “Where are your visualization results?” when noticing that the benchmark script only produced numerical CSV outputs — expecting standard visualization output. AI Initially ran the benchmarks and reported numerical results (Spearman ρ, Precision@K), but did not generate visualizations. Prioritized quantitative metrics over visual output. Difference Analysis: Humans emphasized the importance of visual verification and easily presentable output results for completing analysis. AI defaulted to numerical correctness, considering visualization as an independent optional step.
AI’s Limitations Key Limitation - Due to insufficient CUDA debugging experience, four architecture rewrites were performed before identifying the root cause of cuBLAS large matrix errors (from collecting neighbor data to project processing, from project processing back to collecting data, storing output results in hidden dimensions, using small batch losses). Known GPU driver compatibility issues were not identified early on. The initially designed MCP tool was just a script that wrote files and returned metadata. It wasn’t realized that these tools should directly return content that Claude code could use. When encountering CUDA errors, a conservative CPU training approach was adopted without thoroughly investigating GPU compatibility issues. Multiple attempts were made before recognizing that PyTorch’s CUDA build version did not match the driver version. Initially, it wasn’t recognized that there was a conflict between torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) and PyG’s scattering operations on CUDA. Multiple solutions were tried before identifying the core issue. Without understanding the architectural background, the isolation requirement of MCP server for stdout/stderr was not anticipated. This issue was only realized during the design of the packaging layer, and it wasn’t discovered when exploring the gadget CLI tool initially. General Limitations When a user proposed a large-scale restructuring task (“Organize all documents, merge duplicate functions, archive unused code”), although 3 parallel Explore agents were initiated, the session was interrupted before the agents returned. This indicates there are flaws in handling long-running multi-agent workflows across sessions. WebFetch encountered a 403 error on multiple important papers (bioRxiv scGPT-spatial preprints, ResearchGate links). No alternative strategies such as using archive.org snapshots or allowing users to manually download PDFs were attempted, which limits the depth of literature analysis. When running RM-IDEAL benchmarks, no visual results were generated actively. Users had to explicitly ask “Where are the visual results?” It wasn’t realized that spatial analysis naturally requires visual verification. When supporting bioRxiv/PubMed, it wasn’t asked whether users wanted to modify a 2600-line monolithic file or rearchitect it into modular packages. The implementation was done directly according to user requests without providing architectural alternatives. DNS troubleshooting sessions were not resolved — no sufficient exploration of cluster network configurations was conducted, and no other round-robin distribution methods were proposed. Lessons Learned Key Lesson - RM-IDEAL uses WWL (Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman) graphs to calculate spatial niche matching: By iterating neighbor tag hashing → node feature distribution → optimal transmission between subgraphs (Wasserstein distance), it provides a topological benchmark for evaluating embedding quality.### Cross-Attention for Single Tags Cross-attention on individual tags degenerates into linear projection. Spatial transcriptomics offers a natural solution: k-NN neighbors form a K/V sequence, encoding neighborhood context. This pattern (using spatial neighbors as attention context) applies to various spatial genomics architectures.
PyTorch 2.9 + CUDA 12.9 Issues with cuBLAS on RTX 2080 Ti There are errors in the cuBLAS library for PyTorch 2.9 + CUDA 12.9 on RTX 2080 Ti, leading to illegal memory access issues during large matrix operations. For example, gemm operations of shape 128×1536 fail, and reverse operations with N×N greater than 3500 also fail. Solution: Use small batch processing to keep the matrix size small. The CUDA build version of PyTorch (e.g., cu129) must match the GPU driver’s CUDA version.
scGPT Performs Poor in Zero-Sample Clustering Benchmarks scGPT performs poorly in zero-sample clustering benchmarks (Genome Biology 2025). The AvgBIO score on the Pancreas/PBMC dataset shows that scGPT has a performance range of 0.52-0.60, while HVG baseline scores are 0.60-0.77. scGPT-spatial requires fine-tuning and can only be compared with weaker baseline models (SpaGCN, stLearn), not with the latest models (GraphST, STAGATE). Independent benchmarks are much more reliable than claimed in the original paper.
Conditional Flow Matching for Robot Motion Generation The vector field u_t is learned by linear interpolation of the path x_t = t·noise + (1-t)·actions, from noise to action. A time sampling method with Beta(1.5,1) ensures stability near noise endpoints, and an Euler integration of 10 steps is used for inference. The key insight is that temporal conditioning is achieved through sine embeddings and MLP (or adaRMS for Pi0.5).
Design Principles for MCP Tools Claude Code tools should default to returning content (Markdown reports, JSON data) and optionally performing file persistence. This allows contextual use without traversing the file system. Summaries/research are “enhancing skills,” not just file generators.
stdout/stderr Isolation as a First-Level Design Principle in Packaging CLI Tools for Protocol-Based Systems Isolating stdout/stderr is a first-level design principle, not something considered after the fact. Using context managers allows for clear, reversible redirection without modifying the original code.
Introducing New Abstract Layers (paper_id) and Maintaining Old Field Names (arxiv_id) When Generalizing Single-Source Systems to Multi-Source Systems Auxiliary functions (_paper_id, _paper_url) centralize cross-source logic, making future extensions simpler.
Characteristics of DLPFC Tissue Level Affecting Embedding Sample Matching Layer_1 (sparse, thin) and Layer_4 (granular, densely cellular) show Spearman ρ>0.6 correlations; the most heterogeneous layer, Layer_3, shows ρ<-0.2 correlations. The quality of embeddings is related to the uniqueness of structures and boundary clarity, not just the size of the layers.
RoboTwin Data Format A 14-degree-of-freedom motion space (6 degrees of freedom for left arm + 1 degree of freedom for gripper + 6 degrees of freedom for right arm + 1 degree of freedom for gripper), stored in HDF5 format, with joint_action/vector fields saved and observation values stored as compressed image formats. The Demo_clean file structure includes data/episodeX.hdf5 and instructions/episodeX.json, containing information about seen/unseen language variants.
STAIG Fusion Training for Spatial Genomics UNI2 (1536 dimensions of visual information) + PCA (50 dimensions of genetic information) → STAIG fusion encoder (64 dimensions). Key points: use tau=26 for alignment mode, softmax_log boundary weights, 300 hypothetical clusters, 550 training cycles. If training fails, the model may collapse (all embedding values become zero).
UniVLA Training Process Divided into Three Stages (LAM Phase 1/2, VLA pre-training, fine-tuning for each task). CALVIN training: DiskCalvinDataset loads auto_lang_ann.npy for episode language mapping, reads episode_.npz windows (12-13 frames), extracts rgb_static → LAM → latent_action_idx → VLA input → ActionDecoder → relactions.
Conditionally Running Tools (conda run -n ) Provides clear isolation for sibling projects with dependency relationships. Setting the working directory as the root level of the tool project is important because many Python scripts will use relative imports and sys.path operations.
Production-Level Logging Requires Two Processors StreamHandler (INFO level) for displaying visible progress information, RotatingFileHandler (DEBUG level) for logging diagnostic details. The print() method is retained in interactive UI commands (lists, displays), but all operation/diagnostic messages are transferred to logger.
CALVIN Evaluation Dataset Structure Only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml is needed for environment initialization. During evaluation, episode .npz files are not read (online simulation generates observations). Data extraction reduces the dataset size from 1.3GB to 600KB.
Zero-依赖性API Client (urllib.request + xml.etree) Feasible for Production Tools bioRxiv and PubMed use standard libraries to achieve this, maintaining minimal dependencies.
Python Package Distribution for MCP Server Standard mode provides stateless tools via PyPI and uvx. However, when the server needs to rely on project data directories, pip install -e . (editable installation method) is required. In pyproject.toml, specify [project.scripts], then use Claude mcp add gadget –gadget-mcp to add tools.
Actual Learning Outcomes “When integrating new fusion strategies into existing processes: 1) Register in the apply_fusion() scheduler, 2) Add needs_coords tuple in runner.py and evaluation_planner.py, 3) Add config dictionary items in runner.py, 4) Define experiments in pipeline_config.yaml. This four-step integration approach is standard for all trainable fusion methods.” When updating documentation, use grep to search for CLI symbols and function names, allowing systematic identification of omissions rather than relying on manual code review. Conversation Summary MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ The entire work involved cross-sample analysis, cross-modal enhancement, and literature review 23:31:05.195 | claude_code The comprehensive development work was completed in 6 stages: (1) Using PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion to perform cross-sample RM-IDEAL tests on 151673-151508/151676, revealing high correlations between different layers (Layer_1/4 ρ>0.6), while the heterogeneous Layer_3 showed negative correlation; 7 spatial heat maps and detailed Chinese documentation were generated. (2) A CrossModalEnhancer was designed that uses spatial k-NN neighbors as attention context to avoid degradation issues with individual tags. (3) The CrossModalEnhancer with 3 modes was implemented (approximately 350 lines of code), and integrated into 5 process files; after 4 architectural iterations, the problem with PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS was solved using small batch loss. (4) The CLAUDE.md document was updated to include CrossModalEnhancer, RM-IDEAL commands, and detailed tool descriptions. (5) The structure of the CLAUDE.md document was simplified from approximately 30 lines to about 10 lines. (6) A systematic literature review of scGPT was conducted, revealing that its performance in zero-sample tests was inferior to traditional methods (PCA, scVI). GPU compatibility issues were encountered during cross-sample vision tasks, which needed to be resolved through CPU fallback.
UniVLA ✅ Creating Comprehensive CLAUDE.md Document and Analyzing Training Process 03:35:00.014 | claude_code Used/init skills to explore the UniVLA code library and create the CLAUDE.md document. Recorded build/lint/test commands (torchrun, accelerate, black/ruff), training stages (LAM, VLA pre-training, fine-tuning), evaluation methods (LIBERO/CALVIN/R2R/SimplerEnv), and architecture design (VLM→PrismaticVLM→OpenVLA, visual/LLM base models, action segmentation paths, draccus configuration, RLDS process). Then discussed the requirements of the CALVIN dataset and analyzed the training data process (using DiskCalvinDataset to load auto_lang_ann.npy, episode_.npz files).
OpenPI (Pi0) ✅ Explaining the flow matching implementation in pi0.py 11:37:19.597 | claude_code Users requested an explanation of the flow matching mechanism in policy/pi05/src/openpi/models/pi0.py. Analyzed the implementation of conditional flow matching: linear interpolation of the path x_t = t·noise + (1-t)·actions, time sampling using Beta(1.5,1) from 0.001 to 1, target vector field u_t = noise - actions, training with MSE loss, and reverse inference using Euler integration of 10 steps starting from t=1. Explained the temporal conditioning method using sine embeddings + MLP or adaRMS.
VLA (RoboTwin) ✅ Converting RoboTwin demo_clean to LeRobot format 16:18:03.707 | claude_code Users collected 50 place_dual_shoes data in the demo_clean/ directory through RoboTwin/collect_data.sh. A plan was made to convert the files to Le Robot format (different from the existing zip-based scripts: using 14-DOF instead of 16, extracting directories instead of compressed packages). The convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py script successfully converted 11,459 frames of data. Used LeRobotDataset.create(root=…) parameters to solve the output directory issue and moved the dataset from cache to RoboTwin/data/place_dual_shoes_lerobot/.
CALVIN Environment 🔄 Solving pip installation failure issues and creating dataset extraction script 10:24:15.816 | claude_code Users encountered a DNS resolution failure issue when running calvin/install.sh in the Kubernetes Pod (Errno -3: Temporary Name Resolution Failure). The problem was diagnosed as the domain name server being 10.96.0.10 (cluster DNS), with the proxy set to localhost:9997, unable to resolve pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn or pypi.org. Cluster network issues were confirmed but not resolved. Additionally, the extract_eval_files.py script was created, extracting only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml and metadata from the CALVIN dataset, reducing the file size from 1.3GB to 600KB.
Gadget MCP Server ✅ Converting gadget tools into MCP servers for AI agent discovery 19:34:52.537 | claude_code Designed and implemented the MCP server, providing 9 kinds of gadgets (summary, research, benchmark tests). Created pyproject.toml, .mcp.json, and mcp_server.py files, and implemented stdout capture and SystemExit handling. Fixed the FastMCP version parameter issue, installed the mcp package, and verified that all tools worked properly. Discussed distribution options (PyPI, Smithery). Finally, discussions shifted to returning content to AI instead of defaulting to writing files, resulting in restructuring 5 tools to return complete markdown/JSON content, with an optional save parameter.
Working Space Documentation ✅ Creating CLAUDE.md documents for multi-project working spaces 18:36:35.440 | claude_code Users triggered the/init command. AI launched 5 parallel Explore agents to analyze all projects (CalendarPro, OCR, gadget, get-shit-done, openclaw). Generated a working space-level CLAUDE.md document containing an overview table, quick reference commands for each project, and architecture highlights (EventBus, singleton factory, multi-agent execution, Gateway RPC, etc.).
CalendarPro ✅ Integrating the gadget research/summary tool into CalendarPro 18:59:53.249 | claude_code Users wanted CalendarPro to automatically run the gadget’s research_scout and daily summary functions. After exploration and user feedback, it was decided to use sub-processes and conda run methods instead of MCP. Created 6 new files (protocols, runners, gadget tools, two services) and modified 2 files (configuration, coordinator). Conducted 13 unit tests, all passed. Fixed the issue with the –no-banner conda flag. Updated the CLAUDE.md document by adding new subsystems.
Gadget Research Scout ✅ Comprehensive improvements: logging, multi-source support, and documentation updates 22:09:15.410 | claude_code Improvements were made in 3 aspects: (1) Users requested an explanation of how research_scout worked; AI described a 6-step process, with user feedback that the motivation/innovation fields were empty, requesting logging enhancements and support for bioRxiv/PubMed. (2) Implemented a 5-stage plan: added RotatingFileHandler logging (reducing approximately 77 print statements), implemented zero-field/zero-score warnings, generalized arxiv_id to paper_id while maintaining backward compatibility, added bioRxiv and PubMed search functions (urllib.request, with new dependencies set to zero), and implemented multi-source coordination using the –source CLI flag. The number of files increased from 2155 lines to 2654 lines. (3) Updated the CLAUDE.md document by adding author search functionality (–author flag, search_arxiv_author), init –from-overview function (creating projects based on LLM), and updated the line count.
