summary: Completed the core functions of MIHD spatial transcriptomics: cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation, CrossModalEnhancer for cross-modal enhancement, and scGPT literature research. Promoted several VLA/robot projects (UniVLA document processing, RoboTwin data conversion, process matching analysis). Implemented the MCP server-based tool for integration with CalendarPro, enhancing the multi-data-source support capabilities of research_scout.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-09

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W10 (2026-03-02 ~ 2026-03-08) This week, we have been working across three devices: DCC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. We have been advancing four main areas simultaneously: space transcriptomics research, robot operation training/assessment, AI personal assistants, and paper management tools. In the MIHD project, we completed the full set of experiments for scGPT+UNI2 (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only). We also established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. The Pi0.5 LoRA training achieved an overall success rate of 58.9%, significantly outperforming BC-RNN (0%). Quantitative verification confirmed the advantages of the VLA model. CalendarPro completed its transition from passive response to active decision-making, achieving 321 fully green tests. Meanwhile, we identified and fixed critical integration defects such as BackgroundCoordinator never being started. Gadget Research Scout was developed from scratch to a production-ready state, completing a two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipeline in a single day and providing three research direction suggestions for the first time. error_recovery_benchmark completed the cleanup of 65 symlinks and built infrastructure for visualizing error scenarios in MP4 format. The most important engineering experience this week is that test passing does not mean system availability (integration layers must be verified separately), and it is essential to study the architecture patterns of mature projects before designing an agent-based system.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-02 ~ 2026-03-08 Active Days 7 / 7 Total Conversations 31 Projects Involved 18 Tasks Completed 51 Ongoing Tasks 11 Total Tokens 436,851,629 Total Cost $306.71 Claude Code Tokens 424,137,873 Claude Code Costs $302.72 Codex Tokens 12,713,756 Codex Costs $3.99 Average Daily Cost $43.82 Project Progress error_recovery_benchmark (Robot Error Recovery Benchmark) (5 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Full evaluation of BC-RNN (5 tasks × 50 rollouts). The root cause of SR=0% was identified as an observation key bug, not a model capability issue. Project panorama summary.md was systematically aligned (M14 evaluation data increased from 726 episodes to 6474 episodes. The Pipeline diagram included two paths: VLA Rollout injection and natural failure capture). Cleanup of 65 symlinks (4-way parallel Agents covering 9 stages). All import paths were moved to the error_benchmark framework. 127 unit tests were all green. Visualization of error scenarios in MP4 format for three tasks in coffee/stack/three_piece_assembly. 3 GPUs worked together to produce 9+ videos. Implementation of a pre-error trajectory playback storage layer (action_history + initial_state stored in NPZ), laying the foundation for playback within the first 5 seconds of an error. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The visualization logic for pre-error playback was not completed due to context exhaustion. ⚠️ Formal fixes for BC-RNN’s observation key bug have not been implemented yet. ⚠️ The three_piece_assembly injection mode had zero effective scenarios (the base was too stable). Adjustments were needed in the impact parameters. MIHD (Multimodal Space Transcriptomics Fusion) (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed the full set of experiments for scGPT+UNI2 fusion strategies. QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117%), followed by LLaVA-MLP (0.316). Both were significantly better than scGPT-only (0.170). Three-layer code tracing revealed that STAIG actually did not use gene encoder output (using the original HVG as GCN input). Improvements should start with the GNN structure. Implemented a cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark script (781 lines), added zero embedding runtime detection, and completed a 7-stage evaluation from 151673↔151508. Layer_1/5 performed well (Spearman 0.42-0.66). Negative correlations were found in the intermediate layers (Layer_3/6), indicating limitations in the embedding space of the transition layer. Established a zero-shot differential narrative framework (contrasting with STAIG’s training method). Preparation for Monday’s report was completed. Analyzed QueST’s cross-sample evaluation method (WWL Graph Kernel constructed pseudo ground truth), and wrote documentation for the Cross-Sample Patch Query method (459 lines). Pi0.5 LoRA Training and Evaluation (Tianhe Cluster) (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
BatchedVLAServer completed 9 tasks in parallel evaluations (44 minutes). Overall SR=58.9% (Stack_D0 100%, Stack_D1 95%, StackThree 80-90%). Three-layer evidence confirmed that pi05_base weights were correctly used for all training sessions (config.py weight_loader + training logs + comparison groups). Fixed several key issues in the evaluation pipeline: websocket ping_timeout=None (JIT compilation timeout), JIT concurrency crashes (starting at wrong times). Quantitatively confirmed that VLA inference GPU utilization was only 10% due to action chunking. Multi-client concurrency was 24% slower than serial processing. Continued training on two nodes (an49/an53) for 9 tasks, recovering from the highest step of 18000 without loss. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The optimization plan for BatchedVLAServer batch inference has been designed but not implemented. ⚠️ The rendering of demonstration videos will be executed after confirming the final evaluation results. CalendarPro (Personal Intelligent Butler) (3 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Designed according to OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns, implementing 16 new service files (GapAnalyzer/AutonomousExecutor/SituationMonitor, etc.). 321 tests were all green. Fixed a critical integration defect where BackgroundCoordinator.start_all() was never called (all background services never run in production). Completed the development of Discord Bot Handlers for 9 IntentTypes, eliminating intent routing gaps. Eliminated 16 silent exceptions (except: pass), removed dead code in executor.py and implemented real distribution logic. Externalized 452 utterances to JSON files, enabling UtteranceAugmenter to automatically learn from mismatch logs (run every day at 2 AM). Compiled a complete Chinese tutorial of 1674 lines, identifying three root causes of context-aware bugs in Discord bots. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The solution for context-aware bug fixes has been designed (general_keywords simplification + LLM passing conversation history + semantic routing reset), but has not been implemented. Phoenix/FLARE/zhaoganlong Framework (Robot Training Infrastructure) (3 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
zhaoganlong completed the 4-step pipeline for 9 tasks data preparation (5Hz annotation → speed dataset → LLaVA JSON with 1.3 million entries → RGB rendering of approximately 1 million images). Diffusion Policy (single GPU) and Pi0.5 (2+3 FSDP GPUs) were successfully started training on an53. The Phoenix/FLARE code base was separated from the mixed library into a standalone project, reducing its size from 155GB to 37MB (symbolic links shared for large datasets). Developed 6 full-task training scripts, adding 3 missing OpenPI warmup configurations. Fixed potential issues with norm_stats/prev_actions normalization in Openpi-moe (apply_tree silently skipped). Blocked Items:
⚠️ LLaVA MPM training is blocked due to no network access in the cluster. The local copy of liuhaotian/llava-v1.5-7b is missing. It needs to be confirmed whether the cxp2 user has a complete local copy. gadget/Research Scout (Paper Management System) (2 active days) — ✅ Completed Completed:
Developed Research Scout from scratch, including 6 command-line interfaces, multi-source search on arXiv, and a two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline. First completed the full run, generating three actionable research directions: generative digital twin scenarios, restoring human videos to their original language, and document-guided operations. Implemented a four-level priority configuration for resolve_param(), specialized search for conference papers (arXiv comment fields), and author search (au: query). Implemented early stopping for search deduplication (threshold set at 5 known papers). The init –from-overview option automatically extracts project information from existing documents. VLA-RoboTwin (Robot Operation Data Collection) (2 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed RoboTwin’s Vulkan rendering failures in headless Docker environments. Extracted the user-mode GL library from the official.run package and injected it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH, without requiring root privileges. Developed a system for marking critical regions for 10 robot operation tasks. After finding that critical_region was always zero, changed to recording original ee_target_distances. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The threshold for critical_region needs to be calibrated based on actual distance data before re-collection (τ=0.10m estimate is too low). Key Tasks ✅ Complete Design and Implementation of CalendarPro Personal Butler System Phases 1-3 (2026-03-05) — Designed according to OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns, implemented 16 new services, with 321 tests all green. Identified and fixed a critical integration defect where BackgroundCoordinator never started. ✅ Complete Implementation and Verification of Research Scout’s Two-Stage Paper Evaluation Pipeline (2026-03-07) — Light filtering in Stage 1 → Deep analysis in Stage 2 (up to 20 papers). First run produced three actionable research directions, supporting conference search, author search, and four-level parameter configuration. ✅ MIHD scGPT+UNI2 Full Set of Fusion Experiments (5 Strategies) (2026-03-02) — QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117%), confirmed that STAIG does not use gene encoder output, established a zero-shot narrative framework. ✅ Pi0.5 LoRA Batch Evaluation (9 Tasks, Overall SR=58.9%) (2026-03-03) — BatchedVLAServer completed 9 task evaluations in 44 minutes. Stack tasks were almost perfect (95-100%), BC-RNN was completely green, and VLA advantages were quantitatively proven. ✅ Verification of Pi0.5 LoRA Base Model and Continued Training After 9 Tasks (2026-03-03) — Three-layer evidence confirmed that both sides used pi05_base. Continued training from the highest step of 18000 without loss; prevented false starts by covering existing checkpoints. ✅ MIHD Cross-Sample RM-IDEAL Benchmark Implementation (781 Lines) (2026-03-03) — Two-way cross-section evaluation, added zero embedding runtime detection and automatic recalculation. Layer_3 Spearman r≈0.44, established a reusable evaluation framework. ✅ Determination of the Root Cause of SR=0% in BC-RNN Normal Tasks (2026-03-04) — Code exploration identified an object observation key bug in baseline accuracy evaluation, clearly distinguishing between Normal SR (interrupted by the bug) and Error Recovery SR (core argument of the benchmark). ✅ Fixing Issues with Normativity in Openpi-moe norm_stats/prevactions (2026-03-05) — Found an apply_tree(strict=False) silently skipped missing keys. VAE showed scale mismatch issues between normalized and unnormalized action combinations. Modified compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect and write statistics. ✅ Externalization of Utterances in CalendarPro and Automatic Augmentation by UtteranceAugmenter (2026-03-05) — 452 hard-coded utterances were migrated to JSON files, enabling online augmentation based on automatic learning from mismatch logs, running every day at 2 AM. ✅ Fixing RoboTwin’s Headless Docker Vulkan Rendering Failures (2026-03-06) — Extracted the user-mode GL library from the NVIDIA official 535.104.12 .run package and injected it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH, without requiring root privileges. ✅ 65 Symlinks Cleanup in error_recovery_benchmark and Code Migration (2026-03-08) — 4-way parallel Agents covered 9 stages. All paths were moved from the error_framework to the error_benchmark framework, with 127 unit tests all green. 🔄 Implementation of Critical Region Marking System in VLA-RoboTwin (10 Tasks) (2026-03-08) — Implemented critical_region marking at every frame. After finding that the threshold τ=0.10m estimate was too low, changed to recording original ee_target_distances for data-driven calibration. 🔄 Design of GPU Utilization Optimization Plan for VLA Evaluation Pipeline (2026-03-02) — Confirmed that action chunking was the root cause of only 10% GPU utilization. Designed a BatchedVLAServer batch inference plan, but it has not been finalized. 🔄 Analysis and Fixing Specifications for Context-Aware Bugs in CalendarPro Discord Bots (2026-03-06) — Identified three root causes: general_keywords pre-check truncation following messages, _llm_classify not passing conversation history, and GENERAL routing containing confirmation words. The solution has been designed but not implemented. 🔄 9-Task Data Preparation Pipeline for zhaoganlong and Two-Link Training Startup (2026-03-05) — Completed the 4-step pipeline. Diffusion Policy and Pi0.5 FSDP were successfully started; LLaVA MPM was blocked due to lack of a base model. 🔄 Development of Pre-Error Trajectory Playback Storage Layer (2026-03-08) — Added action_history collection and initial_state storage to rollout_generator. NPZ storage expanded, but the visualization script playback logic was not completed due to context exhaustion. Issues and Solutions 1. STAIG’s two-stage approach actually completely ignores gene encoder output, using the original HVG as GCN input, resulting in scGPT+UNI2+STAIG≡PCA+UNI2+STAIG [MIHD] (2026-03-02) Solution: Three-layer code tracing (Fusion.py→runner.py→STAIGTrainer.py) confirmed the architectural fact: gene encoder was decoupled from GNN training. Improvements should start with the GNN structure rather than replacing the gene encoder.
2. Pi0.5 evaluation client reported keepalive ping timeouts, with all tasks having SR=0%; multi-client concurrency was 24% slower than serial processing [Pi0.5/VLA Evaluation] (2026-03-02) Solution: Add ping_timeout=None to connect() to disable the 20-second timeout (JAX JIT compilation took 30-60 seconds, exceeding the default value). Confirmed that VLA inference was strictly serial, and multi-client concurrency was ineffective. The correct solution is BatchedVLAServer batch inference.
3. STAIG’s fusion embeddings failed in some sections during training, resulting in a NaN Spearman r in cross-sample evaluation [MIHD] (2026-03-03) Solution: Add zero embedding detection in load_fused_embeddings() (norm<1e-6), automatically triggering –recompute_fusion to retrain STAIG for about 50 seconds.
4. Pi0.5 LoRA training configuration names contain ‘pi05_libero’, and users suspect that the initialization weights come from libero rather than pi05_base [Pi0.5/VLA Training] (2026-03-03) Solution: Three-layer evidence (config.py weight_loader field + training log Restoring checkpoint path + comparison group configuration) confirmed that both sides used pi05_base correctly. The configuration name describes the data loading configuration; weight_loader is the only basis.
5. BC-RNN had SR=0% on all normal tasks, which did not match the training rollout [error_recovery_benchmark] (2026-03-04)Solution: The code is exploring ways to fix the bug in the baseline_accuracy evaluation for object observation keys. Strategies that accept empty observations are being implemented. In M14, having an Error SR close to 0% is a core argument of the benchmark (the current strategy lacks error recovery capabilities). Comparing it with Normal SR can help clarify the issue. 6. In the training feature norm_stats.json for Openpi-moe, there is a missing prev_actions key without any errors. In VAE, directly concatenating hist_actions (unnormalized) and actions (normalized) causes scale mismatch [zhaoganlong/openpi] (2026-03-05) Solution: Modify compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect prevActions and write them into the statistics. The fundamental mechanism is to silently skip missing keys when apply_tree(strict=False). New training features must force a synchronized normalization script.
7. All background services of CalendarPro have never been run in production environments. Despite passing all 321 tests, this integration flaw has not been discovered [CalendarPro] (2026-03-05) Solution: Add await coordinator.start_all() in the on_ready() method of discord_bot.py. The root cause is that the registration mode and lifecycle management are decoupled. Unit tests cannot detect silent failures at the integration level.
8. The headless Docker container SAPIEN reports a Render Error. The bare except hides the real error. The apt driver version does not match the kernel module [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-06) Solution: Expose the real error. Confirm that there is a missing NVIDIA Vulkan ICD. Extract the user-mode GL library that matches the kernel version from the official 535.104.12 .run package. Inject it through the VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables (no root required).
9. The initial version of Research Scout performs a single-stage deep evaluation on all 50 papers. There is a significant waste of tokens, and low-relevance papers consume a lot of analysis resources [gadget/Research Scout] (2026-03-07) Solution: Reconstruct it into a two-stage pipeline: Stage 1 for lightweight filtering; Stage 2 for in-depth analysis (up to 20 high-relevance papers). This is based on the reading habits of real researchers.
10. In error_recovery_benchmark, 65 symlinks cause an indirect layer during code migration. After removing the symlinks, the depth of the sys.path parent chain needs to be recalculated [error_recovery_benchmark] (2026-03-08) Solution: The four-way parallel Agents clean up all symlinks according to a nine-stage plan without overlap. The main thread handles Makefile/documents. Fix two unexpected sys.path depth errors (Path.resolve() no longer folds symlinks).
11. In VLA-RoboTwin, the data collected for critical_region is always zero in key areas. The τ=0.10m threshold is purely based on estimates, and it does not match the actual working space scale [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-08) Solution: Do not adjust parameters directly. Instead, record the original ee_target_distances (4-8 distance variables per task). Analyze the real data distribution before calibrating the threshold based on the data.
Learning Outcomes Architecture Three criteria for large-scale implementation: 1) Whether new services are connected to the startup link; 2) Whether cross-layers (IntentType→routing→Handler→service) are updated consistently; 3) Whether silent exceptions (except: pass) mask runtime errors. Passing the test is a necessary condition, not a sufficient condition. Integration layer defects can only be discovered through end-to-end verification. Before designing an Agent-based system, actively study mature projects in the same field. The EventBus/CronScheduler/Plugin registration patterns of OpenClaw and GSD’s STATE.md persistent memory/multi-Agent context isolation are highly reusable architecture patterns. Related fixes should be merged into a single change rather than implemented stage by stage to avoid code rework. apply_tree(strict=False) is an implicit risk in ML training pipelines. If the norm_stats calculation script that needs normalization is forgotten, it will cause a silent scale mismatch that affects training quality. The mismatch records of AI systems are free annotation data. Automatically回流为utterance增广 can achieve low-cost continuous self-improvement. Two-stage information processing (crude screening + fine evaluation) is better than single-stage full-processing in terms of token efficiency and analysis depth. Stage 1 focuses on problem relevance/innovation/relevance to the source (judged within 30 seconds). Stage 2 focuses on key insights that drive results rather than engineering details, and critically examines the baseline and metrics chosen by the author. Debugging Analyze the root cause of low GPU utilization in VLA inference: action chunking makes the inference calls sparse (about 8 times per trial × 2.5s/200s total time = 10%). Multi-client concurrency cannot improve the throughput of strict serial inference. The correct optimization direction is BatchedVLAServer batch inference + multi-GPU independent deployment. Identify the diagnostic path for BC-RNN SR=0%. First, check whether the observation key name/dimension matches the training data. Then, distinguish between “evaluation bug” and “training fails due to defects” based on historical training logs. Avoid misdiagnosing it as a model capability problem. The bare except is the most common debugging obstacle in rendering/simulation frameworks. It should be changed to except Exception: traceback.print_exc(). Parameter settings should be driven by experiments rather than intuition. First, collect a small amount of data to verify the actual distribution before setting thresholds. Incorrect estimates of key parameters (such as distance threshold, driver version) can result in invalid entire batches of data or installation failures. Tools Four-way parallel sub-Agents + the main thread handles non-paralleling parts. The large-scale code migration of 65 symlinks can be completed within a single session. The key is to divide the agent tasks without overlap according to file collections. BatchedVLAServer parallel evaluation (44 minutes/9 tasks) saves about 80% of time compared to serial evaluation. Fix the standard solution for headless HPC nodes with Vulkan/EGL rendering: Extract the user-mode GL library that matches the kernel version from the official NVIDIA .run package. Inject it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/__EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH without rooting. EGL/Vulkan device configuration is independent of CUDA. If CUDA works, Vulkan can also work. The arXiv comment field is actually an area for conference acceptance announcements. A special search for conference papers can be achieved by searching the full text with all:“CVPR 2025” and filtering after finding comments. au:“Author Name” AND (kw1 OR kw2) is an effective way to track a specific researcher’s latest work. Domain Knowledge Learned fusion (QFormer) is significantly better than simple concatenation: scGPT 512d representation itself is valuable, but it needs non-linear projection to be fully activated (+117% vs scGPT-only). The gene encoder of STAIG and the GNN training are decoupled. Differences between documents and code can only be discovered through code tracing. Zero-shot ability is a core competitive point in differentiation with methods that rely on training. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning can achieve 95 - 100% for Stack-type tasks (total 58.9%). BC-RNN fails completely (0%). The complexity of the task (multiple steps, delicate operations) is the main factor determining the success rate difference between VLA and traditional sequence modeling. The Pi0.5 LoRA model also requires at least 2 80GB GPUs (FSDP is a prerequisite). AI Usage Notes Effective Modes:
✓ Multi-layer code tracing (3 - 4 call stacks) to accurately identify the facts of STAIG architecture, providing evidence at the line level. This is more reliable than document analysis. ✓ Four-way parallel Agents are divided into tasks without overlap according to file collections. The large-scale migration of 65 symlinks can be completed within a single session. ✓ sacct step timestamps systematically analyze and correctly exclude misjudgments caused by AI operations, establishing an evidence chain of cause and effect for NODE_FAIL. ✓ Actively clarify vague requirements (whether the author’s search needs LLM evaluation) through AskUserQuestion. This accurately transforms product requirements into engineering solutions. ✓ It performs better than intuition in systematically verifying the correctness of experimental configurations regarding the source of the base model. Limitations:
✗ After implementing new functions, it has not been verified whether they are connected to the system startup link. Only unit tests are relied on, and integration layer checks are omitted (BackgroundCoordinator is not started). ✗ When designing an Agent-based system, mature implementations in the industry were not actively studied. It needs to be referenced after users clearly specify them, such as OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns. ✗ Parameter settings lack experimental verification. The τ=0.10m is purely estimated, resulting in all critical_region label data being zero for an entire batch. ✗ The description of research contributions tends to focus on technical details rather than competitive advantages. The differentiated positioning of zero-shot needs to be clearly specified by users. ✗ The initial task arrangement tends to be conservative and serial. Opportunities for long-term GPU tasks and lightweight visualization tasks that can be executed parallelly are not actively identified. ✗ There is a lack of necessary resume/fresh-start intention confirmation before resource-intensive operations (GPU training). This may lead to the launch of fresh start covering checkpoints. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core priorities: 1) Fix three bugs in CalendarPro’s context awareness. Remove confirmation words/chronological words from general_keywords, pass the last 3 conversation histories to _llm_classify, and reset GENERAL routing utterances. Complete the final loop from architecture upgrade to actual usability of the system. 2) Complete the visualization replay logic for pre-error trajectories (the storage layer is ready. Complete the initial state playback and frame rendering parts in the visualization script). 3) Monitor and evaluate zhaoganlong training results: The Diffusion Policy is expected to be completed in 2 - 4 days. Pi0.5 FSDP still requires about 31 hours. Start batch evaluation immediately after training completion. 4) Unlock LLaVA MPM training: Confirm the integrity of cxp2 user local llava-v1.5-7b. Start training by pointing to the local path through –model_name_or_path. 5) Calibrate the threshold of key areas in VLA-RoboTwin: After analyzing the actual distribution of ee_target_distances, re-set the τ value and collect critical_region label data again. 6) Officially fix the observation key bug in BC-RNN and run normal tasks for evaluation again. 7) Render demonstration videos (compare success/failure rates of Pi0.5 high SR tasks). Research Scout is ready for production and can start configuring regular paper discovery processes for active projects such as MIHD and error_recovery_benchmark.

summary: This week, we conducted parallel efforts across three devices: DCC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. The main projects include space transcriptomics research, robot operation training/ evaluation, AI personal assistants, and paper management tools. The MIHD project completed the full set of scGPT+UNI2 integration experiments (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only), and established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning achieved an overall success rate of 58.9%, significantly outperforming BC-RNN (0%). The VLA model was quantitatively validated to demonstrate its advantages. CalendarPro made a significant leap from passive response to active decision-making, achieving perfect results in 321 tests. Additionally, BackgroundCoordinator’s critical integration defects were identified and fixed. Gadget Research Scout went from zero build to production-ready status within a single day, completing two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipelines and providing three research directions for further exploration. Error_recovery_benchmark completed the cleanup of all 65 symlinks and established infrastructure for visualizing error scenarios in MP4 format. The most important engineering lessons learned this week are: successful testing does not mean system availability (integration layers must be verified independently), and it is essential to study the architecture of mature projects before designing Agent-based systems.

Established an automated research paper discovery and evaluation infrastructure with bilingual output, efficient search functions, and reverse project initialization capabilities for 3 to 5 active projects.” tianhe “Completed Work: Started FLARE D0×4 training (running smoothly, each training took 6.3 seconds). Fixed BC-RNN metric distortion issues through code review. Implemented get_target_pos() function for dynamic target position retrieval during runtime. Achieved 6-task shared generation scripts through task_registry dynamic routing. Obtained LLaVA base models through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion. Resolved the training bottleneck problem caused by ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2+HDF5 preloading.” “Impact: FLARE 4 D0 tasks ran smoothly on an an53 GPU (each task took about 63 hours). BC-RNN evaluation metrics were reliable (all 127 unit tests passed, with GPU verification RP reaching up to 0.800). The 191 error scenarios laid the foundation for subsequent training. LLaVA MPM/MCM training time was shortened from the expected 13.5 days to about 2 days of parallel startup. However, there is an issue with estimating the dataset size, which requires replanning. The Phoenix GPU optimization solution is ready.” Parallel processing on four devices: Completed MIHD documentation system and scGPT research. Started FLARE/Phoenix training and fixed 4 BC-RNN evaluation errors. Implemented Desktop Video web wallpaper function with WKWebView. Batch generated historical reports for more than 20 days in February. Completed CalendarPro context-based intent classification. Developed a complete research proposal management system with language configuration, reverse initialization, and deduplication functions from scratch.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy - ✅ 4 Fixes to BC-RNN Evaluation Process — Corrected the name error of demo_length→demo_len in collector.py. Fixed the mistake of reading trigger_step from scene.dataset instead of scene.detected_by. Fixed the detection logic in the transport stage (added __get_target_pos() function to dynamically obtain target positions). Added a if success: rp=1.0 condition judgment in collector.py. All 127 unit tests passed. GPU verification showed demo_len=605, trigger_step=55, with RP reaching up to 0.800. ✅ Complete Implementation of Desktop Video WKWebView Web Wallpaper — Created WebNavigationHandler.swift file, expanded theContentType enumeration to include.web types, implemented showWeb/cleanupWebView/saveWebBookmark functions, updated the playback state management in AppDelegate, updated the UI of SingleScreenView (URL input field), updated history, thumbnails, localization, and ChangeLog. Added sandbox network permissions, set customUserAgent to modern Chrome UA. “✅ Develop Research Scout System from Scratch — Developed research/research_scout.py script from scratch (about 750 lines of core code), including project CRUD functions, arXiv search (arxiv package), support for three LLM backends (anthropic/openai/claude_cli), paper evaluation scoring, direction suggestion generation, weekly report output (JSON+Markdown), Hugo deployment, and caching mechanism. Designed a two-stage paper evaluation process: first stage quickly screens all papers (title+abstract, judging relevance levels) → second stage conducts in-depth analysis of highly relevant papers (≤20 papers, 3 highlights: insights/ motivation/ experiments/limitations). Completed the writing of requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, and TUTORIAL.md Chinese tutorials.” ✅ Accuracy Update of MIHD CLAUDE.md — Added implementation details for section 3.2 (detector/injector verifier/classifier coordinator, 5 sections) corrected VLA model type (Pi0-FAST→Phoenix/Flare), updated code statistics (script 18→22, test cases 109→122), updated M14 actual evaluation data (726→6474 cases), added information about database stage/severity distribution, explained the process configuration structure, and detailed the cache directory structure. ✅ Create MIHD overview.md Research Positioning Document — Created a 676-line overview.md file, including a one-sentence positioning description (system evaluation of zero-shot embedding of base models in space transcriptomics multi-modal fusion clustering), 4 core questions, 3-stage technical roadmap, ranking of 8 fusion strategies, 8 open-ended questions, search keywords, literature annotation template, quick reference to project structure, and quick start command. ✅ Systematic Research on scGPT/scGPT-Space and Multi-View Performance — Obtained papers on scGPT-space, zero-shot limit papers, BioLLM framework papers. Analyzed internal results of MIHD through 4 parallel proxies. The summary analysis showed that zero-shot scGPT clustering was essentially inferior to multi-view methods (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, a difference of 54%). 🔄 Implementation and Fixing of Desktop Video Browsing Mode and Window Layer — Implemented the browsing mode framework (WallpaperWindow dynamic canBecomeKey, ESC listener program, exit via control panel buttons). The window layer was adjusted multiple times. It was confirmed that the.normal layer could not receive any mouse/keyboard events, so it was used but not fully implemented yet. ✅ Implementation of CalendarPro Context-Aware Intent Classification System — Implemented a context-aware intent classification system (divided general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to avoid incorrect classification due to vague queries like “What time is it?”). Implemented session-aware conversation history management (automatic compression, session reset, token budget control). Completed the A/B/C/D four-phase plan, added 41 special tests, and all 360 tests passed. “✅ Fixing Errors in Recovering Project Configuration — Fixed 3 key errors: mismatch between ID and directory name in project.json, chapter titles in overview.md not conforming to process regular expression patterns, and lack of automatic tagging.” 🔄 Starting FLARE D0×4 Task Training — Ran coffee_D0, stack_D0, stack_three_D0, threading_D0 Pi0.5 LoRA warm-up training on an an53 GPU. Each task had about 36K steps (about 63 hours). The first attempt failed due to PYTHONPATH issues but was successfully restarted after fixing them. Now it runs smoothly, with each step taking 6.3 seconds. ❌ Starting LLaVA MPM/MCM Parallel Training — Confirmed that the paper did not publish LaVA LoRA checkpoints. The cluster lacked a complete llava-v1.5-7b base model. MPM training data llava_motion.json (460MB, 14M lines) was ready. Obtained the base model through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion. Modified lora.sh to use ZeRO-3 to ZeRO-2 to avoid gradient division issues. Started MP- ✅ Generate and improve the CLAUDE.md file for research — Analyzed 1871 lines of the research_scout.py code library, and created a complete CLAUDE.md file containing code organization sections (0-10 chapters), parameter tables, key implementation details, and pipeline function names. “✅ Fixing video transparency issues on desktop and volume synchronization problems — Removed webViewsetValue(false, forKey: ‘drawsBackground’) to fix the transparency issue with WKWebView scroll bars. Added .background(Color(nsColor:.windowBackgroundColor)) in AppMainWindow.swift to fix the transparency issue with the control panel slider. removed the isWebContent guard in setVolume() so that volume/mute controls are visible for all types of content. Fixed the applyWebSettings function to check for global restrictions when switching content for volume synchronization.” ✅ Hugo installation and configuration on Windows — There was an error where ‘hugo’ couldn’t be recognized during the merge step in daily_summary.py. Installed Hugo.Hugo.Extended version 0.157.0 using winget. It was necessary to restart PowerShell to make the PATH environment variable take effect, and finally successfully executed Hugo build and deployment. “✅ Change all ‘weekly reports’ to ‘daily reports’ — There were systematic text replacements in more than 12 places: function names (generate_weekly_report→generate_daily_report), documentation strings, user interface messages, Markdown headings.” Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. BC-RNN RP(0.003) being significantly lower than the random RP(0.020), making the evaluation results completely unreasonable Solution: Code review identified 4 errors that caused metric distortion: demo_len=0 corrupted SPL, trigger_step=0 further corrupted SPL, transport always limited RP to a maximum of 0.5 False, and successful episode RP not being 1.0 led to baseline bias. Fixed field name demo_length→demo_len in collector.py:487, changed collector.py:488 to read trigger_step from scene datasets, env_wrapper.py added get_target_pos() to dynamically obtain target positions, and collector.py added if success: rp=1.0.
Key Insight: The distortion of evaluation metrics is usually not a single error, but a chain reaction of multiple errors. The limit of RP being 0.5 revealed failures in the transmission phase, demo_len=0/trigger_step=0 revealed field propagation errors.
2. WKWebView cannot receive mouse/k keyboard events during browsing mode, even with ignoresMouseEvents=false; the.docdesktopWindow layer causes non-interactive window operations Solution: Set window.level=.normal when entering BrowseMode (not the top level but interactive), and restore CGWindowLevelForKey(.desktopWindow) when exiting BrowseMode.
Key Insight: In macOS, the.docdesktopWindow layer is lower than the Finder desktop icon layer. Windows at this layer do not receive any input events at system level. IgnoringMouseEvents=false has no effect on this layer. .normal(level=0) is the lowest level that is both interactive and does not affect other applications.
3. LLaVA base model download was hindered due to proxy bandwidth bottlenecks (about 50KB/s, downloading 14GB of models took several weeks); GPU nodes could not be directly connected; xet CDN overproxy timed out Solution: Found nyw format llava-1.5-7b-hf (safetensors) in the cluster, and wrote convert_hf_to_llava.py to convert HF format to the original LLaVA pytorch_model.bin format (including vocabulary truncation from 32064→32000). The entire process was done locally without needing a network.
Key Insight: Don’t assume that downloading is necessary. First search for existing model copies on the cluster, and even different formats can be converted. Formatting HF models involves more than just renaming keys; it also involves semantic differences (such as vocabulary size).
4. GPU utilization for Phoenix diffusion strategy training was only 5-6%: DataLoader num_workers=0 caused GPUs to wait for CPU to read data from NFS HDF5 files randomly Solution: Preload all HDF5 data into memory in one go during init method (uint8 type saves 4 times more storage space), close file handles, and then perform all getitem operations on the numpy array. Set DataLoader’s num_workers to 8 with pin_memory=True.
Key Insight: h5py file handling cannot be shared across subprocesses (this is a limitation of PyTorch DataLoader). The only safe solution is to complete loading in the main process and then fork the subprocess. NFS random reading is much slower than sequential reading, and one-time loading is 10 times faster than按需加载.
5. After starting Phoenix MPM training, serious estimation errors were found (actual time was 13.5 days, expected few hours), dataset size estimation error (160K→1M) Solution: The user paused the training and needed to re-plan and optimize it (possibly reducing training steps, increasing GPU count, optimizing data loading, etc.)
Key Insight: Before starting large-scale training, it is necessary to verify the actual dataset size, time per step, and total number of steps to avoid wasting resources. Errors in dataset size estimation can lead to weeks of wasted computing resources.
6. Fuzziness in CalendarPro intent classification was caused by pre-checking general_keywords: ‘What time are you scheduled?’, matching general_keywords with ‘what time’ led to forcing GENERAL, resulting in an error Solution: Divide general_keywords into always_general (always force GENERAL, such as ‘weather’) and context_dependent (force GENERAL only when there is no active session, such as ‘what time’); add confirmation_words(‘ok’, ‘good’, ‘yes’, etc.) to handle quick paths through_is_confirmation_input(); rewritten_process_input() to have a 6-step process
Key Insight: Context-based intent classification requires combining session state (session_id, last_activity_at) and conversation history. Simple keyword matching leads to fuzziness; when there is an active proposal, confirmation phrases (‘ok’) should be routed directly to that proposal.
7. The current research scout paper evaluation process handles all 50 papers at once, wasting resources and lacking in-depth analysis Solution: Designed a two-stage process: the first stage quickly screens all papers (only titles + first 1000 characters of abstracts, classifying high/low relevance), and the second stage deeply analyzes highly relevant papers (up to 20, extracting 3 highlights + detailed scoring + direction suggestions)
Key Insight: Mimicking the paper reading workflow of human researchers (quick screening → deep reading of high-value papers) can significantly improve the efficiency and quality of LLM evaluation, while also generating literature review records for low-relevance papers for future reference
8. Due to the ID (‘robot-manipulation’) in project.json not matching the directory name (’error-recovery-benchmark’), the process could not read overview.md file Solution: Updated the id field in project.json to match the directory name; the process uses project[‘id’] to build the overview.md path
Key Insight: The directory name and ID in project.json must be consistent; _extract_current_methods() uses project[‘id’] to locate overview.md file
9. In the second-stage evaluation, the title of the overview.md section ‘## 3. Current technical approach’ did not match the process’s regular expression pattern ‘##\s*Current method and progress’, resulting in the current method getting a result of ‘(no record found)’ Solution: Changed the title of the overview.md chapter to match the OVERVIEW_TEMPLATE format; removed numbering prefixes and chapter separators
Key Insight: The pipeline uses strict regular expressions for chapter extraction; it must strictly follow the template format
10. FLARE specification statistics calculation failed: After importing openpi in the script, it was not possible to find the installation version of zhaoganlong, nor could the new configuration name of tangzijia be found Solution: Set PYTHONPATH=/HOME/…/tangzijia/shared_deps/openpi/src:$PYTHONPATH in the script to优先加载 tangzijia’s openpi source code, bypassing older versions in the conda environment
Key Insight: When multiple users share the same conda environment, their modifications can lead to import priority conflicts. PYTHONPATH is a minimal intervention solution that does not affect other users
11. WKWebView scroll bars were tracking transparent states (CSS injection to set background color was ineffective) + Sandbox applications could not load any network resources Solution: Removed webView.setValue(false, forKey: “drawsBackground”) to restore the default opaque background, making scroll bar tracking visible; added com.apple.security.network.client=true in the.configurations file
Key Insight: DrainsBackground=false makes the entire WKWebView rendering layer transparent, while CSS’s translucent style on a transparent layer remains transparent. The fix must occur at the rendering layer rather than the CSS layer. By default, the App Sandbox prevents any external network access
12. LLM output contained too much English content; users expect Chinese; unable to configure output language Solution: Implemented a three-tier language system: _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE constant, _language_instruction() generator, and {language_instruction} in all prompts;
Configuration resolution chain is CLI > project.json > config.json > default
Key Insight: Language instructions must be injected at the prompt level, not later in the processing stage; different chapters require different language rules (JSON keys vs. content)
13. Unable to create a project from existing overview.md, unless keywords/categories are manually extracted Solution: Added create_project_from_overview() function, which uses LLM to parse overview.md and extract titles, search_keywords, arxiv_categories, open_questions; then writes to project.json and copies the original overview.md file
Key Insight: LLMs can reliably extract structured metadata from unstructured text; reuse existing call_llm() and parse_json_response() tools
14. When generating reports in bulk, the JSON output generated by LLM often had incomplete or syntax errors, leading to parsing failures Solution: After the user provided the original text, AI manually extracted and repaired JSON fragments (removing non-JSON prefixes/suffixes, fixing quote escapes, adding missing closing parentheses, etc.)
Key Insight: LLMs generate structured output, especially JSON with many nested objects and long text content, which can have formatting errors; automatic repair and verification steps need to be added to the pipeline
General Issues 15. Unable to find Hugo commands on Windows environment, daily_summary.py merge step called website/update.ps1 failed because ‘hugo’ was not recognized Solution: Installed Hugo version 0.157.0 using winget. After installation, it was indicated that a restart of the shell was required. After重启PowerShell, the hugo command worked properly.
Key Insight: On Windows environment, command-line tools installed through winget need to be updated in the PATH before they can be used normally. They cannot be used immediately in the same session
Comparison of Human and Artificial Intelligence Methods Strategic Level BC-RNN RP abnormality diagnosis strategy Role Method Human The user noticed that during M14, BC-RNN SR was close to 0%, which did not match the training progress. The user directly pointed out possible errors in the evaluation metrics and asked for a systematic check of the metric calculation logic | Artificial Intelligence | Initially, artificial intelligence tended to explain that ’error recovery is inherently difficult’, trying to interpret low SR as an expected result rather than prioritizing suspicion of errors in the evaluation code |
Difference Analysis: Humans infer evaluation problems based on normal behavior observed during training, while AI tends to rationalize abnormal data. Human intuition is more accurate, and ultimately identified 4 chain errors that caused metric distortion
Risk assessment before training starts Role Method Human After Phoenix training started, the user immediately noticed serious estimation errors in training time (13.5 days) and decided to stop it to avoid wasting resources | Artificial Intelligence | Before training began, artificial intelligence did not fully verify the actual dataset size (mistakenly treating 160K as the actual 1M), nor did it evaluate the time per step (7.2 seconds/step) and total number of steps (161K), resulting in serious estimation errors |
Difference Analysis: Humans are better at risk control and timely stopping losses, while AI tends to ignore preliminary verification of resource consumption when performing tasks
Paper reading workflow design Role Method Human The user proposed a two-stage reading strategy - a quick screening stage (30 seconds to decide whether to read in depth) and an in-depth understanding stage (finding 3 key points: motivation/ method insights/ experimental design/limitations) | Artificial Intelligence | Initially, artificial intelligence designed a one-time complete evaluation, sending the full abstracts of all 50 papers to LLM for scoring at once |
Difference Analysis: Humans are designed based on the cognitive load of actual researchers. They recognize that quick screening and in-depth analysis require different levels of information. While AI tends to handle things in one go, ignoring the trade-off between tokens cost and evaluation depth
Debugging project configuration errors Role Method Human After the user provided the overview.md path, it asked the AI to ‘fix the format’, but did not diagnose the root cause | Artificial Intelligence | Through code tracing, artificial intelligence discovered 3 interconnected vulnerabilities: incorrect ID, incorrect title formatting, lack of markers |
Difference Analysis: The systematic code analysis by artificial intelligence reveals chain problems that users cannot see; users correctly identified symptoms (“unable to read overview”), but failed to find the root cause
Iterative decision-making process for Window layer solutions Role Method Human Directly rejected the.floating solution from the user’s perspective, and after AI proposed a non-interactive.documentsWindow, decisively pointed out that the.normal layer should be used | Artificial Intelligence | Artificial intelligence tried various technically feasible solutions: first using.floating to ensure interactivity, then rejecting it and instead keeping.documentsWindow, discovering non-interactivity and waiting for user instructions |
Difference Analysis: Humans quickly rejected solutions that were not reasonable from the perspective of user experience and expected behavior. While AI correctly identified fuzziness and proposed problems rather than guessing
Identifying missing language configuration functions Role Method Human The user immediately realized the existence of an UX problem (“too much English in the summary section”) and asked if language could be configured | Artificial Intelligence | Artificial intelligence did not consider language configuration before asking for a solution from the user; only after that implemented a comprehensive solution |
Implementation Level Adjusting the number of FLARE training tasks Role Method Human After AI started 9 training tasks, the user requested reducing the number of tasks to 4 D0-level tasks, clearly indicating that only 4 D0-level difficulty tasks were needed | Artificial Intelligence | Artificial intelligence initially planned to conduct a complete 9-train task (3×D0+2×D1+1×D2), trying to complete all tasks at once |Token Usage Analysis

summary: On the day of research and documentation for multiple projects: The method for evaluating cross-sample queries used in spatial transcriptomics analysis was studied. title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04

content:
Issues and Solutions Key Issues SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial displays a fixed color instead of transparent glass when showing videos.
“Solution: Replace SwiftUI materials with NSViewRepresentable to wrap the NSVisualEffectView, and specify .inWindow mode along with clear backgroundColor in CATransaction.setDisableActions(true).” “Key Insight: SwiftUI’s abstraction layer hides the details of rendering processes. For complex visual effects like window-inner transparency, control at the NS level is necessary.” In 151676, all STAIG fusion embeddings result in zero values (embedding similarity is 0, Spearman r is NaN), indicating a complete model failure.
“Solution: Add a check for np.allclose(embeddings, 0) in load_fused_embeddings. If this condition is met, print a warning and automatically trigger the --recompute_fusion path to re-load the gene/visual embedding data from the cache and retrain STAIG (300 cycles, taking about 50 seconds on RTX 2080 Ti). After fixing, the embedding results return to normal (64 dimensions with a standard deviation of 1.0).” “Key Insight: Due to unstable training, the STAIG fusion embeddings saved in the second phase may become zero. Active detection and automatic re-computation are required in the benchmark script to avoid manual intervention.” When switching videos after selecting one in NSOpenPanel, the old video is still played.
“Solution: Add a mechanism to detect mismatches between bookmark URLs, returning the original URL if there is no match during bookmark parsing, and adding a protection in ensureFileAccess.Solutions: Ensure that FileAccess parses the old video URL as per the bookmark on the screen, rather than requesting a new URL. A protection mechanism has been added to compare the parsed path with the requested path; if they differ, the original URL is returned (this URL has temporary sandbox permissions provided by NSOpenPanel).” Key insights: There is a conflict between the bookmarks stored on each screen and the multi-video history; a protection mechanism is needed to detect that the bookmarks point to expired files.
The success rate of the Coffee task BC-RNN was 0%, suspected to be an evaluation error. Solutions: The training logs were checked, confirming that the Coffee BC-RNN training itself achieved an SR of 0% across all cycles (600 cycles). This was not an evaluation error; the training for this task indeed failed.
Key insights: It is always necessary to verify the training metrics before debugging the evaluation process. A zero value downstream may reflect a failure in upstream training.
The an53 node crashed at 08:30, resulting in the tmux session being lost, and task 46798 was executed. Solutions: An investigation was conducted through the sacct log, determining that the crash was caused by resource overload (8 GPUs were used up to 100%, with high CPU usage). The AI operation was ruled out as the cause (the last completion was at 05:07, 3 hours before the crash). The SSH host key mismatch issue was resolved using ssh-keygen -R an53.
Key insights: Slurm reported minimal details of the crash, but there was no root access to the system logs. The “Request node busy” error in the srun command indicated that the node was overloaded, not a command failure. When using Slurm, even without root access, minimal crash information can be obtained.
The CUDA kernel for P100 GPU (sm_60) is incompatible with PyTorch (sm_70+ version required); torch.cuda.is_available() returns True, but asynchronous operations fail. Solutions: Initially, an attempt was made to use try/except to catch the exception of torchzeros(1).cuda(), but since the asynchronous operations on P100 could not capture errors. The user successfully ran it after switching to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75). Finally, the CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" environment variable was added to force the use of CPU in the script.
Key insights: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks whether the driver and library exist, without checking whether the GPU’s computing power is compatible with the current PyTorch. CUDA operations may fail asynchronously and cannot be caught by Python’s try/except. The most reliable way is to switch GPUs or use environment variables to force the use of CPU.
The JSON generated by the LLM was truncated (…truncated marker), resulting in daily_summary.py failing to parse on 2026-02-14 and other dates. Solutions: The truncated original text was sent as a separate prompt to AI, requesting only the repair/extraction of valid JSON sections; AI successfully restored the complete JSON structure.
Key insights: There is a risk of truncation when the LLM generates very long JSON (especially in scenarios where multiple segments are merged); a truncation detection and automatic JSON repair retry logic should be added during the merging stage, rather than relying solely on manual discovery and intervention.
After the screen saver clock text was updated, there was a 0.2-second afterimage left behind. Solutions: CALayer has an implicit animation of approximately 0.25 seconds for changes in content properties. When updating wrapped blurView.layer?.mask in CATransaction, set setDisableActions(true) to force immediate replacement.
Key insights: By default, CALayer has animations for almost all property changes; explicit transaction control is needed to achieve immediate visual updates.
The BC-RNN strategy evaluation crashed due to “Unable to process data type <class ’numpy.float64>’”. Solutions: Robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() was unable to handle numpy scalar types. The_to_robosuite_obs was modified, only passing keys that contained array/image values to the robomimic strategy.
Key insights: Robomimic expects a structured obs dictionary containing array values; the numpy scalars returned by robosuite (such as gripper_width_float64) must be filtered before being passed to TensorUtils.
The visualization script failed multiple times: Bash heredoc did not conform to != syntax error, file name prefix did not match (151673_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 and filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5), spatial/data path errors (located in Dataset/DLPFC, not directly in the section directory), load_dlpfc_data() returned a value error (returned 1 instead of 2), read_10x_h5 lacked uns[‘spatial’], resulting in sc.pl.spatial() failure. Solutions: Use an independent script/_viz_rm_ideal.py file instead of heredoc; use glob “*filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5” to find the correct file name; directly load sc.read_visium("/path/to/Dataset/DLPFC/151673") (automatically handling the spatial subdirectory); fixed the issue where the unpacked result of load_dlpfc_data was a single return value; abandoned using load_dlpfc_data and used sc.read_visium to ensure the presence of uns[‘spatial’].
Key insights: Bash heredoc is very sensitive to special character escapes; complex scripts should be written as files. The DLPFC data file names have section prefixes; spatial data is located in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial instead of {section}/spatial; load_dlpfc_data used read_10x_h5 instead of read_visium, resulting in a lack of spatial metadata; sc.pl.spatial heavily relies on uns[‘spatial’], and must be loaded using read_visium.
Common Issues The screen saver clock text displayed gray ghosting effects on the edges. Solutions: The root cause was that the font weights between ScreensaverClockHighlight (.light) and renderTextMask (.regular) did not match, causing the mask to thicken and exposing the frosted glass edge of hudWindow. This issue was resolved by unifying the font weight to .regular.
Key insights: The visual effect layer must maintain pixel-level alignment; even a slight difference in weights can expose underlying content.
Processing historical data over 13 days required frequent switching of date parameters and manual confirmation of export/merge status, which was both tedious and error-prone. Solutions: Manually execute the daily_summary.py’s export –date YYYY-MM-DD and merge commands every day, handling each date individually, and confirming the integrity of the output files through the file system.
Key insights: The summarize tool lacks a batch processing mode (e.g., –date-range 2026-02-07..2026-02-19 automatically iterates); in the future, support for batch processing and progress recovery mechanisms should be added.
Using relative paths with nohup failed when starting from the home directory instead of the project directory via SSH. Solutions: Change the command from ‘ssh an53 “nohup bash scripts/…”’ to ‘ssh an53 “cd $PROJ && nohup bash scripts/…”’, ensuring that the working directory is correctly entered before the script execution.
Human and Artificial Intelligence Methodology Strategic Level Discovery of Configuration Errors in Pi0.5 Base Model Setup Role Method Human The user noticed that LoRA fine-tuning used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base as the base model, which was considered a key architectural decision that required verification and possible retraining. Artificial Intelligence During multiple evaluations, artificial intelligence did not actively detect this configuration issue. It focused on monitoring progress and success rates, without questioning the choice of the base model. Difference analysis: Humans used their domain knowledge about model architecture to discover basic training configuration errors. Artificial intelligence ignored this strategic issue while focusing on operational metrics.
Diagnosis of Zero Embedding Issues Role Method Human After discovering that the embedding similarity was 0, immediately asked “Can you show me the visualization results?” shifting from numerical inspection to visual diagnosis; after finding the root cause, requested methods for cross-sample patch queries. Artificial Intelligence Initially attempted a full benchmark test for zero embeddings, only starting troubleshooting when NaN values appeared, without actively requesting visualizations; it was not until the user asked for visualization that a need for spatial heat maps was realized; documentation requires explicit user requests to begin. Difference analysis: Humans actively drove the process from numerical inspection to visualization and then to method summary; artificial intelligence tended to solve individual problems step by step, lacking a global perspective and initiative.
Cause Analysis of an53 Node Failure Role Method Human Humans correctly suspected that AI operations might have caused the failure of an53, requested timeline evidence, and mentioned peer reports about the 11:22 failure time. Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence systematically analyzed Slurm logs (sacct), tracked all srun commands (04:55-05:07, all completed successfully), identified a NODE_FAIL issue at 8:30 (3 hours after AI activity), and diagnosed it as possibly due to resource overload. Difference analysis: Humans used indirect evidence and peer reports to form hypotheses. Artificial intelligence used system logs and timestamps to build a clear timeline, proving that the operation ended 3 hours before the failure.
Design Decisions for Benchmarking Role Method Human Humans explicitly specified testing only the PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION combination, with target chapters being 151673 and 151676. First, they created separate scripts to verify feasibility, then integrated them into subsequent processes. They repeatedly rejected ExitPlanMode, expecting to execute directly when the direction was clear. Artificial Intelligence The initial design covered multiple fusion methods and more chapters; after creating each plan, it tried using ExitPlanMode for approval; due to repeated rejections, it failed to understand the user’s execution style preferences. Difference analysis: Humans made strategic decisions (scope control, priority sorting), while artificial intelligence tended to cover everything comprehensively; humans preferred direct execution modes, and there was a tension between the artificial intelligence’s conservative approval process and the user’s expected execution efficiency.
Batch Processing Strategy for Historical Data Role Method Human Humans knew they needed to generate historical reports for 13 days, manually called the tool to process each date, and continued processing the next date when issues occurred. Artificial Intelligence As a daily report analyst, artificial intelligence passively responded to each individual date request, reprocessing dialogues, extracting information, generating reports each time, without realizing that this was a batch task, nor did it actively suggest workflow optimization. Difference analysis: Humans had a global intention for batch processing and time planning capabilities, while artificial intelligence could only see individual calls; artificial intelligence lacked the ability to actively identify repetitive patterns and suggest automation improvements.
Screen Saver User Experience in Video-Free Situations Role Method Human Users expect the screen saver to remain active without video loading, displaying a black screen and a clock (consistent with the system’s screen saver). Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence initially only saw the screen saver without video as an intentional design; it did not question the user experience assumption until it was explicitly requested. Difference analysis: Humans applied a psychological model from the macOS system screen saver; artificial intelligence followed literal code constraints, without inferring broader user experience expectations.
Judgment of Granularity in Merging Multiple Dialogues | Role | Method | |——|—-| | Human | Users explicitly request to analyze multiple dialogue segments on the same day separately (possibly from different devices or projects), and then merge these segments into a complete daily report to maintain logical clarity. | | AI | AI strictly follows the instructions, analyzing each segment separately before merging them. However, this leads to redundancy in the merged output (repeating similar issues rather than truly summarizing). Humans need to emphasize “merge and refine, not copy verbatim” in their prompts. |
Difference analysis: Humans understand that “merging” is about information aggregation and deduplication, while AI tends to retain all details, resulting in an output that is lengthy. AI needs explicit instructions to achieve the quality of summary that humans expect.
Artificial Intelligence Limitations Key Limitations During multiple evaluations, failed to actively detect configuration errors in the pi0.5 base model (the difference between libero and the base model), until the user explicitly raised this issue. “Lack of initiative and global perspective: ① Did not proactively propose a visualization solution, which required explicit user requests; ② Did not proactively suggest writing method papers, which also required user requests; ③ After multiple ExitPlanMode failures, still failed to understand the user’s execution style preferences (direct implementation rather than waiting for approval); ④ After discovering zero embedding issues, failed to actively diagnose the root cause (why the embeddings saved in the second phase would crash).” “Error in the design of the compute_embedding_similarity function: It incorrectly called compute Embedding_similarity(embeddings_ref, niche_mask_query=None) in run_cross_section_benchmark, with incorrect parameters and confusing logic; it was only fixed after recreating the compute_cross_embedding_similarity function. This indicates a lack of deep understanding of cross-section similarity calculations.” “Multiple failures in Bash heredoc and path handling: ① Bash heredoc != escaped with != (syntax error); ② Multiple misses of DLPFC file names with partial prefixes; ③ Mistakenly thought that spatial/ was located directly in the sub-directory, not in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial; ④ Misuse of load_dlpfc_data() resulting in a lack of uns[‘spatial’]. This indicates a lack of understanding of the DLPFC dataset file organization and scanpy API.” “Error in the CUDA compatibility detection strategy: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks drivers and libraries, not GPU computing power compatibility; try/except cannot catch CUDA asynchronous failures; in the P100 asynchronous failure scenario, multi-layer CPU backtracking was ineffective. This indicates a lack of understanding of PyTorch CUDA mechanisms and asynchronous error propagation.” As a daily report analyst, repeatedly called to handle similar tasks (analyzing historical dialogues → generating structured reports), but failed to recognize it as a batch processing scenario, nor did it actively suggest “whether batch processing mode is needed” or “automated scripts” to improve efficiency. The API token expired multiple times during the sub-agent exploration task, hindering investigations of checkpoints and zhaoganlong folders. Manual intervention/log-in was required. Before attempting to resolve the BC-RNN numpy dtype error, multiple debugging iterations were conducted (trying to track backtraces, ssh retries, checkpoint checks), and it was ultimately determined that to_robosuite_obs was the focus for filtering scalar types. When seeing that the SR in the coffee evaluation was 0%, failed to actively check the training logs; before investigating the upstream training failure, a clear issue statement was needed. General Limitations### Codex 模型 输入 输出 缓存创建量 缓存读取量 成本 占比 claude-opus-4-6 27,751 34,899 522,679 6,916,902 $7.7364 73.6% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 36,614 63,117 1,096,922 10,517,205 $2.7751 26.4% Codex 概述 指标 数值 总令牌数 12,713,756 输入令牌数 12,648,899 输出令牌数 64,857 推理令牌数 30,268 缓存读取量 12,095,872 总成本（美元） $3.9926 模型细分 模型 输入 输出 缓存创建量 缓存读取量 成本 占比 claude-opus-4-6 27,751 34,899 522,679 6,916,902 $7.7364 73.6% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 36,614 63,117 1,096,922 10,517,205 $2.7751 26.4% ——- —— —— ——— ———— —— ————- gpt-5.3-codex 12,648,899 64,857 30,268 12,095,872 $3.9926 100.0%
summary: On the DCC platform, the implementation of the MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark test was completed (including design, 781 lines of script code, error fixing, visualization processing, and 459 lines of documentation). Additionally, data from 13 days of historical AI conversation logs was processed using a MacBook, resulting in structured daily reports. The BC-RNN/Pi0.5 evaluation process was also built, and some key model configuration issues were identified on the Tianhe platform. Finally, the interface was optimized, and relevant documentation for the website was prepared.
