summary: We have identified and fixed several critical errors in the MIHD benchmark tests. These errors were caused by the limitations of the STAIG architecture. In addition, we have compiled a detailed list of external dependencies, and we have diagnosed the bottleneck problem in GPU usage during the evaluation process.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W09 (2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01) This week, we continued to work on two main tasks: space transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark on both DCC and Tianhe clusters. On DCC, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, which improved ARI by 44.4%. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a large-scale code refactoring involving approximately 250K lines of code. On Tianhe, we established the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline with 9 tasks running in parallel. We identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in the Pi0.5 evaluation task, and obtained key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation (the learning strategy had an SR of approximately 0% under error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning). Additionally, we successfully launched the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 with stable performance.
Week Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01 Active Days 5 / 7 Total Conversations 19 Projects Involved 5 Tasks Completed 35 Ongoing Tasks 6 Total Tokens 177,973,151 Total Cost $105.52 Average Cost per Day $21.10 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark (Error Recovery Benchmark) (Active for 5 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
M14 three-way evaluation (Pi0/Pi0.5/BC-RNN) completed: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random was the highest, indicating that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios. This directly proved the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation: The training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Complete implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline: Image mode configuration rewritten to match the MimicGen paper. 9 tasks ran in parallel smoothly, and the Stack D0 achieved an SR of 64% after 22 epochs. Fixed defects in Coffee BC-RNN configuration: Added a missing 57-dimensional object observation key, fixing the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. Completed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline: Fixed a bug in the 4-layer continuous image format, generated 11 tip_over scenarios and MP4 visualizations. Successful launch of Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks running in parallel (6×A800, GPU utilization 100%, 77.7GB per card). The evaluation framework was extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, with a new task registry and unified management of the –task parameter. Evaluation of the Pi0.5 Phoenix 9-task base model: Total SR=4.2% (Stack D0 24%, others ≤2%). Discovery of a database containing 649 scenarios: ~519 compatible scenarios + ~130 natural_* incompatible scenarios. The actual effective evaluation target was ~519. Blocked Items:
⚠️ Initialization issues in the Coffee environment caused objects to be distorted or stuck (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. MIHD (Multimodal Space Transcriptomics Fusion) (Active for 3 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed the critical weight loading bug in scGPT: The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and NMI improved by 33.3%. STAIG fusion RM-IDEAL evaluation led the competition (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). This proves that multimodal fusion is much more effective than single-modal baseline in capturing spatial niches (avg r≈0.06-0.12). Full support for Visium HD crop10large: 8 files were modified, and 4 path adaptation bugs were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end (vs PCA 0.086). Discovery of VisiumHD barcode mapping (r_big//4 pattern), and comparative visualizations of pathologist annotation, STHD cell type, and STAIG fusion clustering. Comparison experiment between Vision Refine: It was found that scan_cluster refine is beneficial for weak fusion but harmful for strong fusion. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). Large-scale refactoring of the repository in 6 phases: 41 files/~250K code and 5.3MB images were removed. 6 functional modules were extracted, and 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. The bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed. Three CPU acceleration implementations for STAIG fusion (vectorization of cdist + GPU-based random number generation + batch forward. Expectation is a 20-50x improvement). Blocked Items:
⚠️ There were issues with objects being distorted or stuck during the initialization of the Coffee environment (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. Key Tasks ✅ Fixed the scGPT use_fast_transformer weight loading bug and re-extracted all embeddings (2026-02-28) — The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). All 17.7% attention weight parameters were randomly initialized. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 186/186 parameters matched. ✅ Identified the root cause of OOM in Pi0.5 full fine-tuning and switched to LoRA architecture (2026-02-23) — It was confirmed that JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage but does not reduce activation memory. The complete training state of Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU on A800 or higher. Switching to gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora reduced memory to ~22.5GB. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step. ✅ Completed the three-way evaluation of M14 and confirmed key conclusions (2026-02-27) — BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This proves that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios, directly supporting the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. ✅ Cleaned and refactored the MIHD repository in 6 phases (2026-03-01) — In Phase1, 41 dead files (~250K code + 5.3MB images) were removed. In Phases 2-4, the run_benchmark.py shared functions were moved to 6 modules and the bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed (>= replaced with <). 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. ✅ Full support for Visium HD crop10large (8 files modified) (2026-02-28) — The data_loader/clustering/run_benchmark/pipeline modules were modified. Automatic clustering without annotations for HD was added, and HD-specific visualization logic and hd_global configuration sections were added. The coordinate space alignment and double preprocessing issues were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end. ✅ Discovered and fixed compatibility issues with M14 scenarios (2026-02-23) — All three-way evaluations crashed on scene 122. AI analysis using EnvironmentMismatchError found that the 649 scenario database contained two types of environments (~519 compatible + ~130 natural_* incompatible). The collector.py added a try-except for scene-level to fix this issue. There were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios. 🔄 Launched the parallel fine-tuning pipeline for Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks (2026-03-01) — The train_pi05_benchmark.py was created (5 subcommands). 18 configurations were registered to openpi/config.py. Four launch bugs were fixed (JAX_PLATFORMS, norm_stats path, boolean parameters, W&B proxy). It successfully launched on 6×A800 with 100% GPU utilization and 77.7GB per card. 🔄 Completed the script implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline and launched 9 tasks in parallel (2026-02-28) — The train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py was implemented based on the hyperparameters from the MimicGen paper. Three library-level bugs were fixed (mujoco_py import, MimicGen environment registration, get_bounding_box_half_size inheritance chain). 9 tasks ran in parallel on a single GPU (~2.2GB VRAM per task), with 50 rollouts evaluated every 20 epochs. ✅ Located and fixed the missing object observation keys in Coffee BC-RNN (2026-03-01) — By comparing the actual obs key structure of the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee lacked a missing 57-dimensional object state (Pod/Machine/Holder position + relative pose + hinge angle). Extra_low_dim=[‘object’] was added for all 9 tasks. This fixed the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. ✅ Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation (2026-02-26) — It was confirmed that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Humans could directly identify this obvious issue through a single question, while AI analysis failed to detect it immediately. ✅ Rewritten BC-RNN image mode configuration (5 tasks, consistent with MimicGen paper) (2026-02-26) — The bc_rnn_configs were rewritten from all low_dim to image mode: ResNet18+SpatialSoftmax encoder, CropRandomizer 76×76, RNN hidden_dim 1000, batch_size 16, epoch_every_n_steps 500. This was consistent with the main results in the MimicGen paper. ✅ Achieved three CPU acceleration implementations for MIHD fusion training (2026-02-23) — scipy cdist vectorization replaced O(n²) nested loops (expected a 100-500x improvement). Random number generation was moved to GPU to eliminate the transmission overhead per epoch. QFormerFusion added a batch forward interface (expectation is a 20-50x improvement). ✅ Developed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (2026-03-01) — The bug in the 4-layer continuous image observation access of robomimic BC-RNN was fixed. 11 tip_over error scenarios and MP4 visualizations were generated. ✅ Completed the full evaluation of Section 151673 RM-IDEAL (27 methods × 7 layers) (2026-02-28) — The RM-IDEAL evaluation with 27 different embedding methods was run. 189 three-dimensional panel visualizations were generated. staig_fusion had an average r of 0.396, ranking first (peak r of Layer_3 was 0.644). This quantified the advantage of multimodal fusion. ✅ Extended the evaluation framework to include 5 MimicGen tasks (2026-02-26) — 5 new task YAML configs and task_registry.yaml were created. The –task parameter was added to the 1/3/4 scripts. The observation dimension error caused by _D0 env suffix stripping was corrected from 65 to 37. run_full_eval.sh was rewritten to support multi-task loops. ✅ Completed the full benchmark test of STAIG fusion (11 DLPFC slices) (2026-02-23) — pca+uni2+staig_fusion and none+uni2+staig_fusion were run on 11 DLPFC slices. The UNI2 patch_size compatibility bug was fixed (256×256 → 224×224). 10/11 slices were successful, with an average ARI of 0.546. Section 151676 had known collapses. ✅ Completed the comparison experiment between MIHD Vision Refine and baseline (2026-02-23) — On slice 151673, a full comparison was run for 8 fusion methods with/without scan_cluster refine. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). It was found that refine was beneficial for weak fusion (attention +0.086) and harmful for strong fusion (-0.054). ✅ Fixed key bugs in evaluate_mimicgen.py and collector.py (2026-02-23) — The bug causing in-place array modification of quat2axisangle was fixed (using np.clip+copy instead). The collector.py’s scene-level EnvironmentMismatchError catching was fixed. The reproducibility of env.seed() was ensured, and the state dimension 8D was verified by assertion. Problems and Solutions 1. Pi0.5 full fine-tuning requires ~62GB/GPU. JAX FSDP=4 cannot effectively reduce memory. Repeated OOM in [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Switch to the gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora architecture, which reduces training parameters by ~90% and reduces memory to ~22.5GB/GPU. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step.
2. All three-way evaluations in M14 crashed on scene 122/649: ~130 natural_* scenario xml_hash incompatibilities with the current mimicgen environment caused EnvironmentMismatchError, leading to a complete evaluation process crashing [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Add a try-except for EnvironmentMismatchError in collect_on_scenes() of collector.py. Skip incompatible scenarios and record warnings. Resume the process with –resume. It was confirmed that there were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios.
3. Training logs had serious distortions due to stdout buffering: Pi0.5 training showed only ~580 steps, while actually reaching step 3000. This led to misjudgment of training termination and unnecessary restarts [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Standardize the addition of PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1+stdbuf -oL in the training script and sbatch submission scripts. For long-term training, use sbatch to submit independent batch jobs and decouple them from the interactive session lifecycle.
4. The success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation was 0%, which was misjudged as a model quality issue [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: It was confirmed that the root cause was that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task (Coffee/Stack/Threading/Assembly).
5. Observation dimension mismatch in BC-RNN evaluation (Expected 65, got 37) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: Add import mimicgen to create env.registry during create_env(). Do not strip the _D0 suffix anymore. Keep all MimicGen observables (the _D0 environment provides additional object state observables).
6. In scGPT, TransformerModel.init omitted self.use_fast_transformer, causing key remapping in load_pretrained() to never execute. This led to random initialization of 17.7% attention weights. When strict=False, this was silently ignored [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: Add a line of self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer to model.py. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 11 DLPFC slices’ embeddings were re-extracted.
7. WebSocket client always encountered ConnectionRefusedError. Attempting different address formats (localhost/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0) failed [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28)Solution: It was found that the http_proxy environment variable was set to 127.0.0.1:10087. The websockets library failed to connect through this proxy. After unsetting all proxy variables at the beginning of run_eval.sh, the connection succeeded. 8. The CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacks the get_bounding_box_half_size() method. An AttributeError was reported during the rollout initialization of the MimicGen Coffee task due to an interface gap between MimicGen and the current robosuite version. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28) Solution: The entire call chain (coffee_machine.py → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject) was traced back. The get_bounding_box_half_size() method was implemented in each of the three different levels of base classes.
9. In HD data, the uns[‘spatial’] images appear to be full-sized but their coordinates are in the fullres pixel space after cropping. This mismatch leads to visual misalignment. Additionally, since X has been logged transformed, the pipeline will again perform normalize_total + log1p, resulting in a double transformation. [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: In load_hd_data(), replace uns[‘spatial’] images with cropped_fullres.tif and recalculate the scalefactor. Add a skip_log parameter to preprocess_data(). For HD data with skip_log=True, only perform HVG filtering.
10. The Coffee BC-RNN loss converged normally (-7.66→-15.4), but the SR remained at 0% for a long time. Tasks configured with the same parameters achieved 64% after 20 epochs. This performance is highly similar to that of models with insufficient capacity. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee requires a 57-dimensional object state (required for multi-stage precise operations). The configuration template lacked this field. Add extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all 9 tasks and regenerate the configuration.
11. There were four consecutive bugs when accessing image observations with robomimic BC-RNN: The logic for enabling the camera, the checkpoint config in JSON string format, issues with obtaining image resolution, and problems with converting from HWC to CHW formats. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Check the rgb modality from the checkpoint config JSON string (which requires json.loads). ② Read the actual 84×84 size from shape_metadata. ③ Manually perform HWC→CHW transposition and conversion from uint8 to float32/255 normalization (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process_obs in standard rollouts).
12. There were four consecutive bugs when starting Pi0.5 LoRA training: ① Setting JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu alone was ineffective. ② The norm_stats path was incorrect. ③ The boolean parameter –overwrite=False was ineffective. ④ Access to W&B network interfaces on HPC nodes was blocked. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② The actual path for norm_stats is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. ③ Use –no-overwrite instead of –flag=False. ④ Preset WANDB_MODE to disabled.
13. Adapting the pipeline path for VisiumHD HD data: Failure occurred when finding the image path for the vision encoder and loading spatial coordinates during the fusion stage, as it assumed the DLPFC directory structure. [MIHD] (2026-03-01) Solution: Add a cropped_fullres.tif pattern recognition to find_spatial_image(). Pass crop_dir.parent as data_root when calling the vision encoder. In the fusion stage, directly load coordinates from data.obsm[‘spatial’] without looking up the path.
14. When calculating norm_stats, the GPU util value was 0, but the memory was fully occupied. The task did not progress for a long time. The root cause was that PaligemmaTokenizer eagerly loaded all visible GPUs when calling get_config().model. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously to force the CPU mode (norm_stats does not require model inference at all). Add CPU parallelism and sub-sampling (max-frames 10000) to reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes.
Learning Outcomes Architecture JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage, which does not affect the loading of the complete model during initialization or forward activation memory. The full training state for Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU, and a single A800 cannot handle full fine-tuning with 80GB. LoRA (gemma_2b_lora) is the only feasible solution for the A800 cluster, reducing the memory requirement to ~22.5GB. Switch to LoRA when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. The observation keys configured for BC-RNN must match the task complexity: Tasks with multi-stage precise operations like Coffee require a 57-dimensional object state. A single 84×84 image cannot provide sufficient spatial precision. The performance of configuration bugs (normal loss but SR=0) is highly similar to insufficient model capacity. It is necessary to distinguish between them by comparing dataset structures. Vectorization and batching significantly accelerate the process: O(n²) nested loops can be reduced by about 100-500x through cdist matrix operations. Python’s loop iteration can also be accelerated by about 20-50x through batch GPU forward operations. Norm_stats calculations do not require model inference at all. Forcing the CPU mode, sub-sampling, and parallelism can reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes. Methodology for restructuring large repositories: Systematically grep to verify all imported dependencies, then execute in the order of “creating new modules → updating references → deleting old files”, and perform a dry-run verification. Before merging functions with the same name but different variants, carefully compare the boundary conditions (>vs>=vs< can lead to completely opposite results). Debugging PyTorch model.load_state_dict(strict=False) will silently ignore non-matching keys. Keyed weights may run for a long time with random values without causing any errors. Production code should actively print missing_keys/unexpected_keys and verify the statistical values of parameters. There may also be similar bugs in the official open-source repository. It is necessary to audit the weight loading path actively rather than blindly trusting it. Key details of robomimic BC-RNN inference: The checkpoint config is stored as a JSON string (which requires json.loads, not direct dict access). When bypassing the standard rollout process, manual HWC→CHW transposition and uint8→float32/255 normalization are required (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process obs in standard rollouts). Domain Knowledge Key conclusions from M14: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This directly proves the necessity of LoRA fine-tuning in M15. The 649 scenario database consists of two types of environments: ~519 impulse/augmented compatible scenarios (xml_hash matching) and ~130 natural_* natural capture scenarios (xml_hash different). There were approximately 519 effective evaluation scenarios in M14. All document and target episode numbers need to be updated accordingly. STAIG fusion is overall leading in the RM-IDEAL evaluation (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). Multimodal fusion has a much better ability to capture spatial niche structures than single-modal baselines (gene/vision average r≈0.06-0.12). The effect of Vision Refine is bipolar: It is beneficial for strong fusion (attention +0.086 ARI), harmful for weak fusion (-0.054 for qformer). Strong fusion already has the ability to handle high-dimensional inputs, and reducing dimensions actually results in a loss of information. Cross-task evaluation is a common pitfall in robot learning: If the training set task does not overlap with the evaluation task, it directly leads to 0% SR. It is necessary to ensure consistency between train/eval tasks during the experimental design phase. The MimicGen _D0 environment provides additional object observables. Removing the _D0 suffix from observations reduces the dimensionality from 65 to 37, disrupting the input consistency during reasoning. VisiumHD coordinate mapping: 2um bin of r_big//4 results in an 8um grid row (similar for col). The visualization size parameters of scanpy spatial are multiples of spot_diameter_fullres. For HD with 8μm diameter (diameter≈7.3), the size should be approximately 4.0. For DLPFC with a diameter of approximately 144, use size=1.0. The Leiden community found that resolution=1.0 is the default clustering strategy for unlabeled HD data (crop10large k=17, Silhouette=0.302). Tools Best practices for Slurm cluster training projects: For long-term training, sbatch should be used instead of srun to avoid session timeout due to SIGTERM. All training scripts should be standardized with PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL. Before starting the job, use fuser /dev/nvidiaX to clean up zombie GPU processes (monitoring tools like nvitop also hold CUDA contexts). MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID corresponds to the physical GPU number, not the logical CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES number. Key configurations for JAX/Pi0.5 training: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95 is effective for A800 with 80GB (from 61 to 77.7GB). ③ HPC nodes must have WANDB_MODE set to disabled. ④ The argparse boolean flag –no-overwrite cannot be used instead of –flag=False. ⑤ The openpi norm_stats path is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. The HTTP/HTTPS proxy environment variable (http_proxy) will be transparently proxied by the Python websockets library to establish WebSocket connections. When debugging local services on HPC clusters, it is necessary to unset the proxy variables. The MimicGen environment registers with robosuite through the side effects of importing mimicgen. It is necessary to explicitly perform this import before calling external tools. It is necessary to trace back the entire inheritance chain and implement methods that are missing in third-party libraries in all base classes. Only fixing the point where it is called most recently will cause other subclasses to still report errors. The pam_slurm_adopt cluster’s GPU nodes do not support direct SSH access. srun –overlap can attach a new step to an existing interactive job to reuse node resources. Robomimic does not have a TTY environment, and will exit with EOFError if it encounters an existing checkpoint directory. It is necessary to completely clean the directory before starting each time. The checkpoint directory is the most reliable evidence of training completion. A SLURM job crashing does not mean that the training has failed. Notes on AI Usage Effective Modes:
✓ Systematic code auditing to identify root causes: By comparing the key values in checkpoints and model state_dicts, it was possible to accurately locate the bug in ScGPT weight loading (just one line of omission). By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, a configuration defect in Coffee was discovered independently (without any human intervention). ✓ Complete call chain tracing: Fixed the issue where CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacked a method by tracing the three-level inheritance chain (coffee_machine → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject). Implement it in all base classes instead of just fixing the point where it is called most recently. ✓ Progressive debugging: Resolved Pi0.5 training OOM issues by analyzing step by step (downgrading dependencies → patching source code → switching architectures → submitting sbatch jobs). ✓ SubAgent parallel monitoring: Monitored multiple long-time experiments through TaskOutput in the background and advanced the different projects across DCC/Tianhe clusters in parallel. Limitations:
✗ Misjudged the memory usage principle of JAX FSDP (thought that FSDP=4 could be reduced to ~16GB). Only switched to LoRA after several OOMs. It was necessary to take immediate action when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. ✗ Failed to identify the most obvious reason for the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 during diagnosis. Listed secondary factors such as OOM/action clipping first, and was directly questioned by users, hitting the key point. ✗ Lack of active long-term task health checks: Abnormalities such as GPU idling (util=0 but full memory) and processes getting stuck relied on users to discover them actively, rather than AI auditing them. ✗ lacked preventive verification before integrating external tools: Did not check the availability of mclust/rpy2, image format conventions (HWC vs CHW), outbound network restrictions (W&B), and zombie GPU processes before multiple failures were fixed. ✗ Attempted to automatically call ExitPlanMode to proceed after writing the plan several times (was rejected by users 2-3 times). AI failed to understand that a plan is a decision-making checkpoint for users, not an automatic progress signal. ✗ Lacked active verification of outdated dialogue cache information (such as Pi0.5 step 5000 crash records). It was necessary to rely on the actual checkpoint file status rather than dialogue records. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core tasks: ① Wait and analyze the results of the LoRA 9 task fine-tuning for Pi0.5 (expected to be completed in 53 hours). Compare it with the 4.2% base model baseline to verify the general improvement effect of LoRA on MimicGen tasks. ② Progress the training of BC-RNN 9 tasks to 600 epochs, focusing on the convergence of complex tasks such as Coffee/ThreePieceAssembly D1. ③ Fix the simulator bug in the Coffee environment initialization (object misalignment/crashing), and complete the closed-loop evaluation of Coffee BC-RNN. ④ Start the M15 experiment design: Based on the conclusions from M14 (learning strategy error scenario SR≈0%), plan the data collection strategy for LoRA fine-tuning in error scenarios, and determine the sampling ratio and data volume of error types. ⑤ Expand MIHD: Run multimodal benchmark tests on more VisiumHD slices to verify the statistical stability of STAIG fusion. Also conduct a special investigation into the training collapse of DLPFC 151676 slices (adjust tau/dropout rate/figure construction parameters).

summary: This week, we have been working concurrently on two projects within the DCC and Tianhe clusters: Space Transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark. On the DCC side, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, resulting in an average improvement of 44.4% for ARI tasks. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a major refactoring of the code involving approximately 250,000 lines of code. On Tianhe, we established a new BC-RNN Phoenix training pipeline with 9 parallel tasks. We identified and fixed the root cause of the task distribution mismatch that led to zero success rates in the Pi0.5 evaluation. This led to key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation: the learning strategy achieved SR≈0% in error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Additionally, we successfully stabilized the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 hardware.

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-01

content:
Monthly Summary — 2026-02 February 2026 marked intensive parallel progress across spatial transcriptomics research and robotics error recovery. The month began with STAIG multimodal fusion breakthroughs (ARI improvement from 0.09 to 0.5435), followed by critical coordinate system fixes boosting vision-only performance from ≈0 to 0.22-0.25. MIHD achieved systematic debugging of cascading architecture issues (gene dimensionality, 64-dim embedding bottlenecks, vision encoder coordinates, scGPT checkpoint loading), established self-supervised learning baselines (SCAN/STEGO/BYOL), and extended to Visium HD. Error Recovery Benchmark expanded from 121 to 649 scenarios, integrated VLA natural error capture (150 scenarios, 3x efficiency), and discovered fundamental policy failures (0-0.28% success rates across BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5), empirically justifying Phase II human teleoperation data collection. Infrastructure advances included two-phase benchmark pipelines (86% computation savings), RM-Ideal spatial evaluation, public crowdsourced submission systems, and multi-device cloud sync. Recurring patterns revealed implementation-reality gaps, cross-repository dependency fragility, and AI’s tendency toward symptom mitigation over root cause analysis.
Monthly Overview Metric Value Active Days 22 / 28 Total Conversations 99 Projects 29 Tasks Completed 115 Tasks In Progress 23 Total Tokens 871,155,704 Total Cost $579.93 Claude Code Token 819,719,637 Claude Code Cost $563.56 Codex Tokens 51,436,067 Codex Cost $16.37 Daily Average Cost $27.62 Project Progress MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics (19 days active) — 🔄 active Dominated February with 19 active days. Month-long systematic debugging uncovered and resolved multiple cascading issues: gene feature dimensions (3000-dim vs 50-dim PCA), coordinate system three-layer mappings (hires scale factor, barcode loss, X/Y swapping), 4 STAIG implementation differences, 64-dim embedding bottleneck, and scGPT uninitialized attention weights (9.45M parameters). Established comprehensive evaluation beyond ARI/NMI: spatial structure metrics (RM-Ideal with 8min→0s optimization), self-supervised learning baselines, and Vision Refinement two-stage fusion framework. Expanded to Visium HD with automatic cluster estimation. STAIG confirmed as optimal fusion method (avg r=0.396, Layer_3 peak 0.644).
Key Milestones:
Fixed STAIG implementation achieving 0.5435 ARI (123% improvement from 0.202 baseline) Vision encoder three-layer coordinate bug fix (ARI: ≈0 → 0.22-0.25) scGPT checkpoint loading bug fix (+44.4% ARI improvement) Diagnosed 64-dim embedding bottleneck causing 91% cluster collapse Implemented RM-Ideal spatial niche matching evaluation with WWL graph kernel Redesigned two-phase pipeline reducing vision encoder recomputation by 86% Image-only self-supervised clustering baselines (SCAN ARI 0.303, +20.6%) STAIG fusion CPU optimization (100-500x speedup, 2-5s → 10ms) Visium HD full-stack adaptation with automatic cluster estimation Completed 7/11 DLPFC sections benchmark with quantified metrics visualization Error Recovery Benchmark (Robotics) (12 days active) — 🔄 active Achieved breakthrough quantitative justification for specialized error recovery training. Early month resolved multi-detector interaction bugs (global cooldown masking, distance gating conflicts, stub functions, EEF body localization). Mid-month established comprehensive Phase II infrastructure with 16-day execution plan and validity framework (dataset scale, taxonomy justification, baseline evaluation). Late-month M14 results proved fundamental task misalignment: Pi0.5 0% success due to training on MimicGen vs. evaluation on PickPlace. Final evaluation across 649 scenarios × 4 policies quantified complete failure (BC-RNN 0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 both 0%, Random baseline highest at 0.0199 recovery_progress), empirically validating necessity for M15 human teleoperation data collection and fine-tuning.
Key Milestones:
Expanded database from 121 to 649 scenarios (M5/M6 scene generation) Fixed 4 cascading PreGrasp detector bugs (0→2 scene triggers) Integrated VLA policy with natural error capture (150 scenarios, 3x efficiency) Implemented 24-type error classification system across 3 families M14 baseline evaluation revealed 0-0.28% success rates across all policies Pi0.5 Phoenix LoRA fine-tuning completed (99999 steps on 5 MimicGen tasks) BC-RNN image-mode training launched across 9 tasks in parallel Discovered Pi0.5 training-evaluation task mismatch root cause Phase II roadmap restructured with M12-M15 milestones gadget Toolchain (4 days active) — 🔄 active Infrastructure expansion focused on crowdsourcing, multi-device workflows, and operational reliability. Built relay-based submission architecture for untrusted contributor ingestion with validation/audit trails. Migrated from single-machine Anthropic API to multi-device tool supporting Claude CLI + rclone sync with config resolution chain (CLI→env→config.json→defaults). Fixed ccusage LiteLLM model name mismatch causing claude-opus-4-6 $0 cost reporting. Completed historical report backfill and architecture design for enhanced daily report readability with per-conversation narratives.
Key Milestones:
Implemented public benchmark submission pipeline with GitHub Pages relay architecture Added Claude CLI backend and rclone multi-device cloud sync Fixed ccusage billing errors and token statistics with fallback pricing Batch backfill of 9 days historical daily reports (Feb 8-16) Deploy subcommand implementation for automated Hugo posting Designed conversation summary feature architecture CalendarPro (1 days active) — ✅ completed Single high-density session. Extended intent recognition to support time-range batch deletion with robust fallback chain (LLM parsing → regex extraction → user clarification). Corrected API endpoints to minimize OAuth scope requirements. Integrated Discord UI approval workflow for destructive batch operations. 68 tests passing.
Key Milestones:
Implemented batch delete with natural language parsing and LLM fallback Fixed Google Calendar 403 permission errors via correct API endpoints Added Discord UI confirmation for batch operations QueST Integration (2 days active) — ✅ completed Ported and adapted QueST’s spatial niche matching algorithms to MIHD framework. Implemented Wasserstein-Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernel with shared hash tables across query/reference subgraphs, node ID offset collision prevention, and k-hop caching. Established foundation for spatial structure evaluation beyond traditional clustering metrics. Precision@K vs SameLabel@K measure different properties—ranking quality vs type retrieval.
Key Milestones:
Implemented WWL graph kernel RM-Ideal scoring with optimal transport Created niche query evaluator with k-hop subgraph caching (8min→0s speedup) Validated on DLPFC with Spearman r=0.42, SameLabel@50=1.0 Desktop Wallpaper (macOS App) (1 days active) — ✅ completed macOS desktop video wallpaper application maintenance. Addressed critical gaps in history tracking and power-saving modes that were previously non-functional due to stub implementations returning false. Implemented automatic versioning infrastructure.
Key Milestones:
History feature implementation with 100-entry limit and thumbnail generation Multi-screen power save mode fixes (4 stub functions implemented) Automatic build number updates via PBXShellScriptBuildPhase Key Achievements STAIG Strict Mode Performance Breakthrough (2026-02-07, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Identified and fixed 4 critical implementation differences between MIHD and original STAIG (image normalization omission, patch size, StandardScaler removal, CPU random masking), achieving ARI 0.5435 on section 151673—surpassing original benchmark and resolving month-long performance mystery. 64-dim Embedding Bottleneck Root Cause Analysis (2026-02-16, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Diagnosed STAIG fusion cluster collapse (94% of spots in 2 clusters instead of 5) as cascading failure: 64-dim bottleneck compressing 1536-dim UNI features by 96% (std=0.12), then spatial majority voting eliminating minority clusters. Other fusion methods using 256-512 dims don’t exhibit this pathology. MIHD Vision Encoder Three-Layer Coordinate Bug Fix (2026-02-17, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Diagnosed and fixed three cascading bugs: (1) hires coordinate space mismatch (×scale_factor≈0.15), (2) barcode loss due to integer indexing, (3) X/Y column swapping in tissue_positions_list.csv. Fix boosted visual single-modality ARI from ≈0 to 0.22-0.25, validating that patch extraction was previously sampling only background regions. scGPT Checkpoint Loading Bug Fix (2026-02-28, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Identified that TransformerModel.init failed to save self.use_fast_transformer attribute, causing 17.7% of parameters (9.45M attention weights) to remain randomly initialized during load_pretrained. One-line fix boosted DLPFC full-set ARI by +44.4% (0.1177→0.1700) and NMI by +33.3%. M14 Baseline Evaluation Reveals Fundamental Policy Failures (2026-02-23 to 2026-02-27, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Completed 649-scene × 4-policy evaluation revealing BC-RNN 0.28% success rate (5/1800), Pi0/Pi0.5 both 0%. Random baseline achieved highest recovery_progress (0.0199), quantitatively proving all learned policies fail worse than random actions in error scenarios, establishing necessity for M15 recovery-specific training. Pi0.5 Training-Evaluation Task Mismatch Root Cause Analysis (2026-02-26, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Discovered that Pi0.5’s 0% success rate was due to fundamental task misalignment: model trained on 5 MimicGen tasks but evaluated on PickPlace (not in training set). Established task-aligned evaluation framework across 5 MimicGen tasks, resolving the root cause of apparent model failure. VLA Natural Error Capture Pipeline (2026-02-16, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Successfully integrated Pi0 VLA model with natural error capture, generating 150 scenarios in 50 rollouts (3x efficiency vs injection mode). Identified dominant natural failure modes: joint_limit_approach (47%), grasp_wrong_pose (30%). Total benchmark scenarios increased from 121 to 271 across 8 error types. Two-Phase Benchmark Pipeline Redesign (2026-02-14, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Architected and implemented caching-based pipeline separating embedding extraction (Phase 1) from fusion evaluation (Phase 2). Reduced UNI2 recomputation from 77 times to 11 times (~86% savings). Phase 1 completed 11 sections in 30 minutes on RTX 5000 Ada. RM-Ideal Spatial Evaluation System (2026-02-08, MIHD/QueST Integration) — Implemented WWL graph kernel + optimal transport-based RM-Ideal scoring for niche query evaluation. Achieved 8min→0s speedup via k-hop subgraph caching. Initial validation on 151508 Layer_3: Spearman r=0.42, SameLabel@50=1.0, establishing spatial structure evaluation beyond traditional clustering metrics. Image-Only Self-Supervised Clustering Implementation and Baseline (2026-02-19, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Implemented STEGO/BYOL+GAT/SCAN self-supervised clustering methods. SCAN achieved best image-only performance (ARI 0.303, +20.6% vs baseline 0.251), while SCAN+llava_mlp multimodal fusion reached ARI 0.409. Established that pure vision-only ST spatial domain clustering is a literature gap. STAIG Fusion CPU Three-Level Optimization (2026-02-23, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Achieved 100-500x speedup via scipy cdist vectorization (O(n²) loop elimination, 2-5s→10ms), GPU-based dropout (eliminated epoch-level CUDA sync), and batched QFormer forward (2700 sequential→1 batch call). Full DLPFC evaluation showed STAIG as optimal method (avg r=0.396, Layer_3 peak 0.644). Visium HD Full-Stack Adaptation with RM-IDEAL Evaluation (2026-02-28, MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics) — Implemented 8-step HD support: load_hd_data() with cropped image replacement, estimate_n_clusters_leiden() for automatic cluster estimation, HD-aware visualization. Validated on crop10large (17,502 spots, k=17, Silhouette=0.302). Ran 27-method × 7-layer RM-IDEAL evaluation confirming STAIG superiority. Recurring Problems 1. Implementation-paper discrepancies causing silent performance degradation (5 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-01, 2026-02-05, 2026-02-07, 2026-02-16 Root Cause: Code reproduction from papers involves subtle architectural choices (normalization presence/absence, dimension sizes, activation placement, coordinate system mappings) that dramatically impact results but are rarely documented explicitly. Default assumptions (e.g., ‘standard ViT preprocessing = ImageNet normalize’) often contradict actual paper implementations. Requires line-by-line comparison across code/config/data preprocessing. Status: ✅ Resolved
2. Environment dependency mismatches and cross-platform compatibility issues (14 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-01, 2026-02-05, 2026-02-14, 2026-02-17, 2026-02-20, 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-26, 2026-02-28 Root Cause: Multi-conda-env projects with non-overlapping dependencies; Windows-specific subprocess behavior; rpy2 lifecycle management in repeated calls; robosuite/robomimic/MimicGen evolve APIs independently with incompatible signatures; datasets>=4.0 breaks LeRobot 0.1.0 column access; MimicGen environments require explicit import mimicgen side-effect for registration; robosuite adds ‘-state’ suffix to modality keys at runtime but HDF5 stores raw keys. Status: 🔧 Workaround
3. GPU cluster resource management and configuration issues (12 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-17, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-21, 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-27, 2026-02-28 Root Cause: Multiple independent configuration dimensions (SSH stability, SLURM partition permissions, GPU memory visibility, EGL device binding, residual process VRAM occupation, proxy env vars blocking localhost) with non-obvious interactions. pam_slurm_adopt policy prevents direct SSH to compute nodes. MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID must match physical GPU ID not relative index. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
4. Documentation-code divergence with stale metrics and missing updates (4 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-02, 2026-02-03, 2026-02-16 Root Cause: Human-readable logs (experiments_log.md, CLAUDE.md) manually updated separately from code registries and CSV data. No automated sync between ground truth (DETECTOR_REGISTRY, ErrorType enums, CSV files) and narrative documentation. Generated visualizations not part of standard pipeline execution. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
5. Cascading failures from shared global state in plugin architectures (2 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-14 Root Cause: Multi-detector systems using global cooldown counters and distance gating create hidden cross-detector dependencies. One detector’s activation silently blocks others; stub implementations in one code path mask working implementations elsewhere. Error injection framework has orthogonal ‘when to trigger’ (detector) vs ‘which injector type’ (injector) dimensions—framework must independently enumerate injector alternatives. Status: ✅ Resolved
6. Third-party API usage assumptions contradicting actual library behavior (3 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-14, 2026-02-16 Root Cause: Using low-level APIs manually instead of high-level wrappers (openpi’s create_trained_policy() vs manual norm_stats loading); assuming fixed output formats (ccusage dict vs list); insufficient inspect.signature() verification before integration. Documentation often incomplete/outdated. PyTorch strict=False model loading silently skips mismatched keys, leaving parameters randomly initialized. Status: ✅ Resolved
7. AI defaulting to symptom mitigation over root cause analysis (6 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-19, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-26, 2026-02-28 Root Cause: AI prioritizes fastest path to green tests: disabling failing components (Coffee rollout), skipping unmatched checkpoint keys (strict=False), bypassing detector logic. Lacks architectural preemption—discovers structural defects (friction/gripper_bias producing 0 scenes, vision encoder complete failure ARI≈0) only after runtime failure. Requires explicit human directive to pursue deeper fixes. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
8. Overly optimistic ‘completion’ criteria missing integration validation (3 occurrences) Dates: 2026-02-16 Root Cause: Equating ’tests passing’ with ‘system operational’ without end-to-end validation using real dependencies (model checkpoints, external APIs, hardware). Synthetic unit tests validate code correctness but miss integration issues like camera image passing, observation format mismatches, GPU OOM. Status: 🔄 Ongoing
Human-AI Collaboration Trends Human-initiated insights: 36 items AI limitation patterns: Lacks proactive experimental quality validation—missed vision encoder complete failure (ARI≈0) after 66 experiments by only checking file counts AI limitation patterns: Symptom-first debugging: spent 3 BC-RNN iterations on type/dimension errors before user identified robosuite base.py ‘-state’ suffix root cause AI limitation patterns: Environment compatibility pre-diagnosis insufficient (7 occurrences across Windows subprocess, rpy2, CUDA env vars) AI limitation patterns: Static code analysis misses cross-module integration bugs (detector-injector orthogonality, EEF body name lookup) AI limitation patterns: Over-engineering solutions when simpler domain-specific approaches exist (4 occurrences) AI limitation patterns: Documentation-code consistency not proactively verified—code registries/enums are ground truth AI limitation patterns: No cross-session memory: user recalls modifications but AI cannot locate across devices/conversations AI limitation patterns: Architecture blindness: failed to preempt friction/gripper_bias detector-injector orthogonality gap until 0 scenes produced AI limitation patterns: Over-relies on structured tools (ExitPlanMode, AskUserQuestion) when users want natural dialog and architectural control AI limitation patterns: Resource awareness gaps: defaults to single-GPU allocation on 8×A800 node, misses residual process VRAM occupation, sets wrong EGL device IDs Improvement areas: Proactively run end-to-end integration tests with real dependencies before claiming completion—’tests passing’ ≠ ‘system operational’ Improvement areas: Implement proactive result sanity checks (embedding uniqueness, loss trends, coordinate alignment, per-feature std >0.01) before declaring tasks complete Improvement areas: When debugging, always construct and validate root cause hypotheses before iterating fixes—avoid symptom whack-a-mole Improvement areas: Check code registries/enums as ground truth when updating documentation; grep all old values after edits to ensure complete cleanup Improvement areas: Use inspect.signature() and source code reading for third-party API integration; always prefer high-level wrappers over manual low-level assembly Improvement areas: Identify and question domain assumptions (e.g., ‘standard preprocessing’) against actual paper implementations via line-by-line comparison Improvement areas: Recognize when human provides architectural insight vs. when AI should execute systematic analysis Improvement areas: Track user’s known-good configurations and flag deviations (e.g., warn when training/eval environments diverge) Improvement areas: Develop heuristics for ‘analysis vs. execution’ task boundaries from user rejection patterns of ExitPlanMode Improvement areas: For GPU clusters: systematically check existing allocations → residual processes → env vars → dependency versions before launch Improvement areas: Ask clarifying questions about experimental priorities (depth-first minimal validation vs. breadth-first comprehensive coverage) before defaulting to batch execution Monthly Learnings Digest Architecture (architecture) Embedding dimension bottlenecks cause catastrophic collapse: STAIG’s 64-dim output compressing 1536-dim vision features by 96% (std=0.12) combined with spatial majority voting eliminates minority clusters. Other methods using 256-512 dims avoid this. Cascading failures compound: poor embeddings → refinement amplifies rather than fixes errors. SCAN-refined embeddings show divergent effects: beneficial for simple fusion (mean +5.5%, attention +8.6%), detrimental for learnable fusion (qformer -5.4%)—learned fusion handles high-dim noise better, prefers original 1536d over 256d refined. (Source: 2026-02-14, 2026-02-16, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-20, 2026-02-23) Spatial coordinate systems in Visium data have three mapping layers (full-res → hires×scale_factor≈0.15 → lowres). tissue_positions_list.csv columns are counter-intuitive (col4=pxl_row/Y, col5=pxl_col/X). Vision encoder fixes proved patch-level coordinate validation is critical—wrong coordinates caused 100% background sampling. Statistical validation: use known array_row/col physical step lengths vs. pixel change amounts to unambiguously determine CSV column meanings. Embedding quality validation must include: unique row count, per-feature std >0.01, pairwise cosine similarity mean. (Source: 2026-02-17, 2026-02-19) Large-scale benchmark design pattern: cache at minimal reuse granularity (encoder×section), not full experiment combinations. Phase 1 extracts embeddings once per encoder-section pair; Phase 2 all fusion experiments load from cache. Reduces redundant computation 80%+. Training-type fusions (contrastive, Q-Former) need separate timeout/epoch configs from inference-type fusions. Error injection framework design principle: ‘when to trigger’ (detector) and ‘which injector type’ (injector) are orthogonal dimensions—framework must independently enumerate injector alternatives. (Source: 2026-02-14, 2026-02-17, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-22) Error classification system architecture: two-layer design with rule-based classifiers (primary, state-threshold-based, fast deterministic) covering observable state space + VLM layer (auxiliary, renders video, natural language descriptions, cross-validation) for high-level semantics and ambiguous boundaries. Plugin architecture with base ABC + registry enables extensibility. Multi-detector systems require per-detector lifecycle management (independent cooldown, distance gating) to prevent silent cross-detector blocking. (Source: 2026-02-14, 2026-02-16) Idempotency design patterns: embed processed-item tracking in data structures (_merged_devices list in export JSON, _source_device field at end for tail readability). Python 3.7+ dict insertion order preservation is reliable. LLM-in-the-loop requires complete fallback chains: LLM parsing → regex extraction from _raw_input → user clarification. Never assume data field populated by LLM in SR-only paths. (Source: 2026-02-14) Debugging (debugging) Implementation-paper alignment requires line-by-line comparison, not high-level matching: image normalization presence/absence, activation on final GCN layer, patch sizes, edge weight directions, coordinate system mappings. Default assumptions (‘standard ViT preprocessing’) often contradict papers. Root cause analysis workflow: systematic comparison (code + config + data preprocessing) across three sources (original implementation, new implementation, paper description) prevents missing subtle differences. PyTorch strict=False model loading is double-edged: allows partial weight matching but silently skips mismatched keys. scGPT bug (self.use_fast_transformer not saved) caused 17.7% parameters (9.45M) to stay random, boosting ARI +44.4% after one-line fix. (Source: 2026-02-01, 2026-02-05, 2026-02-07, 2026-02-28) Environment compatibility debugging: Windows npm commands are .cmd files invisible to subprocess list mode (need shell=True); Claude CLI detects nesting via CLAUDECODE/CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRY env vars (clear in subprocess); rpy2 activate()/deactivate() must pair in try/finally blocks to prevent converter stack corruption in loops; CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG must set before all imports for deterministic CUDA. (Source: 2026-02-05, 2026-02-07, 2026-02-14) Integration testing discipline: ’tests passing’ ≠ ‘system operational’. Synthetic unit tests validate code correctness but miss real dependency issues (camera image passing, model checkpoint loading, GPU OOM). Run end-to-end with actual hardware/APIs/checkpoints before claiming completion. For multi-step tasks, persist plans to files (markdown with nested goal structure) for cross-session continuity and user review. File existence + row count checks are insufficient—validate embedding unique row count, per-feature std, pairwise cosine similarity. (Source: 2026-02-12, 2026-02-16, 2026-02-17, 2026-02-19) Domain Knowledge (domain) Image-only ST spatial clustering has fundamental ceiling: DLPFC ARI 0.11-0.16 (vs. multimodal 0.45-0.64) due to foundation model cancer-tissue training bias, brain cortex layer morphology subtlety, patch-level encoding lacking spatial context. SCAN self-supervised clustering optimal for image-only (ARI 0.303, +20.6%). Spatial transcriptomics evaluation beyond clustering metrics: RM-Ideal (WWL graph kernel + Wasserstein distance) captures niche similarity at spatial structure level. Implementation requires: shared hash tables across query/reference subgraphs, node ID offset to prevent collisions, k-hop subgraph caching (8min→0s). (Source: 2026-02-08, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-20, 2026-02-23) VLA task-specificity and cross-task generalization limits: Pi0.5 0% success on PickPlace was task misalignment (trained on MimicGen Coffee/Stack/Threading, evaluated on PickPlace not in training set). Pi0.5 Phoenix on MimicGen shows task-dependent performance: Stack_D0=24%, Stack_D1=12%, Threading_D0=2%, Coffee/StackThree≈0%—simple stacking learned, contact-intensive complex tasks fail. Natural error capture (natural_capture mode) is 10x more efficient than synthetic injection (3 scenarios per rollout vs 0.3). Robot benchmark validity requires four-layer proof: dataset scale + taxonomy meaningfulness + baseline performance + data improves foundation models. M14 results quantify necessity: all learned policies fail worse than random (BC-RNN 0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 0%, Random 0.0199). (Source: 2026-02-16, 2026-02-18, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-26, 2026-02-27, 2026-02-28) OSC controller dynamics fundamentally change force injection behavior: kp=150 stiffness causes controller to recompute compensation forces in each physics substep, potentially negating external forces within single env.step(). Visualization requires neutral actions (zero vector) to prevent trajectory tracking. MuJoCo xfrc_applied clears every step—continuous force requires per-step reapplication. EEF body name is ‘gripper0_eef’ not in common lists; fallback substring search risks false matches. (Source: 2026-02-14, 2026-02-16) Tools (tools) Third-party ML library integration best practices: always use high-level API wrappers (e.g., create_trained_policy()) rather than manual low-level assembly; verify actual signatures with inspect.signature(); read example code in source repos as documentation is often incomplete. Observation preprocessing must handle multiple input formats with robust key lookup and fallbacks. Dependency version pins: LeRobot 0.1.0 incompatible with datasets>=4.0 (must pin datasets<4.0). MimicGen envs register via import mimicgen side-effect; stripping _D0 suffix loses extra observables. robosuite adds ‘-state’ suffix to modality keys at runtime but HDF5 stores raw keys. (Source: 2026-02-16, 2026-02-20, 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-26, 2026-02-28) JAX FSDP limitation: init_train_state() passes partial_params with replicated_sharding, so every GPU needs full model params during initialization regardless of final sharding strategy—this causes OOM even with FSDP. Pi0.5_base full fine-tuning needs >62GB/GPU; LoRA reduces trainable params 90% (VRAM ~22.5GB), making 80GB A800 viable. JAX multi-GPU auto data-parallel via CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, no code change needed. (Source: 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23) Multi-device toolchain design pattern: config resolution chain (CLI flag > env var > config.json > default), per-device export tracking for idempotency (_merged_devices list), atomic writes (temp file + os.replace), cloud sync via rclone (headless-friendly, 70+ services, single binary). Claude CLI backend enables API-free operation on servers. Defense against format variations (ccusage dict vs list). Crowdsourced data pipeline security model: three-tier architecture (untrusted submit → trusted gateway → protected storage) with validation/audit trails. (Source: 2026-02-06, 2026-02-14) GPU cluster deployment checklist: (1) check existing allocations/residual processes via nvidia-smi, (2) unset proxy env vars (http_proxy blocks localhost WebSocket), (3) MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID = physical GPU ID (not CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES relative index), (4) SLURM: use srun –jobid –overlap to attach to existing job when sbatch partition permissions unavailable, (5) long SLURM jobs: PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL + set -eo pipefail + ERR trap for monitorability. A800 80GB can run 9 BC-RNN training concurrently (~2.2GB each). Lustre distributed filesystem du scanning extremely slow—use parallel background tasks + –max-depth=1. (Source: 2026-02-21, 2026-02-22, 2026-02-23, 2026-02-27, 2026-02-28) Performance optimization patterns: O(n²) vectorization via scipy cdist (nested loops 2-5s → 10ms, 100-500x speedup). GPU-based adaptive_dropout_adj eliminates epoch-level CUDA sync overhead. QFormer batched forward: convert 2700 sequential spot calls to 1 batched GPU parallel (20-50x speedup). Dense contrastive loss (InfoNCE) with large batch (n_spots~3600) + small temperature (0.07) prone to float32 overflow—need clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax + L2 norm input. Patch extraction: move np.array(pil_img) for 20000×20000 image outside loop (1x vs 3500x conversion). (Source: 2026-02-07, 2026-02-23) Documentation verification workflow: code registries/enums (DETECTOR_REGISTRY, ErrorType) are ground truth, not docstrings or prior docs. After edits, grep all old values to ensure complete cleanup. Visualization must be explicit pipeline step, not assumed side effect of data generation. Automated sync between CSVs and markdown logs prevents divergence. Matplotlib + scanpy integration: use ax= parameter with explicit fig, axes = plt.subplots() instead of save= parameter to retain figure control. summary: February 2026 marked intensive parallel progress across spatial transcriptomics research and robotics error recovery. The month began with STAIG multimodal fusion breakthroughs (ARI improvement from 0.09 to 0.5435), followed by critical coordinate system fixes boosting vision-only performance from ≈0 to 0.22-0.25. MIHD achieved systematic debugging of cascading architecture issues (gene dimensionality, 64-dim embedding bottlenecks, vision encoder coordinates, scGPT checkpoint loading), established self-supervised learning baselines (SCAN/STEGO/BYOL), and extended to Visium HD. Error Recovery Benchmark expanded from 121 to 649 scenarios, integrated VLA natural error capture (150 scenarios, 3x efficiency), and discovered fundamental policy failures (0-0.28% success rates across BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5), empirically justifying Phase II human teleoperation data collection. Infrastructure advances included two-phase benchmark pipelines (86% computation savings), RM-Ideal spatial evaluation, public crowdsourced submission systems, and multi-device cloud sync. Recurring patterns revealed implementation-reality gaps, cross-repository dependency fragility, and AI’s tendency toward symptom mitigation over root cause analysis.
